ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عُقْبَةَ بْنَ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ ‏ ‏أَوْ سَمِعَهُ مِنْهُ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ خَصَّهُ بِهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏نَكَحَ ‏ ‏ابْنَةَ أَبِي إِيهَابٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ أَمَةٌ سَوْدَاءُ قَدْ أَرْضَعْتُكُمَا فَجِئْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لَهُ فَأَعْرَضَ عَنِّي فَجِئْتُ فَذَكَرْتُ لَهُ فَقَالَ فَكَيْفَ وَقَدْ زَعَمَتْ أَنْ قَدْ أَرْضَعَتْكُمَا فَنَهَاهُ عَنْهَا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj informed us, ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿUbaydullāh ibn Abī Mulaykah informed us: that ʿUqbah ibn al-Ḥārith ibn ʿĀmir informed him — or he heard it from him, if he did not narrate it to him specifically — that he married the daughter of Abū Ihāb. A black slave woman said: 'I breastfed you both.' He came to the Prophet ﷺ and mentioned that to him, but he turned away. He came again and mentioned it. He said: 'How [can you continue] when she has claimed she breastfed you both?' So he forbade him from her.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْبٍ ‏ ‏وَعَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُقْبَةَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أُتِيَ ‏ ‏بِالنُّعَيْمَانِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏ابْنِ النُّعَيْمَانِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ سَكْرَانُ قَالَ فَاشْتَدَّ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَ مَنْ فِي الْبَيْتِ أَنْ يَضْرِبُوهُ فَضَرَبُوهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ فَشَقَّ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَشَقَّةً شَدِيدَةً ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عُقْبَةُ ‏ ‏فَكُنْتُ فِيمَنْ ضَرَبَهُ ‏
en: Sulaymān ibn Ḥarb and ʿAffān narrated to us, they said: Wuhayb ibn Khālid narrated to us — ʿAffān said in his narration — Ayyūb narrated to us, from ʿAbdullāh ibn Abī Mulaykah, from ʿUqbah ibn al-Ḥārith: that the Prophet ﷺ was brought al-Nuʿaymān — or Ibn al-Nuʿaymān — while he was drunk. It grieved the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ greatly — ʿAffān said in his narration: It distressed the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ severely — and he ordered whoever was in the house to beat him, and they beat him. ʿUqbah said: I was among those who beat him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَتَى ‏ ‏كِظَامَةَ ‏ ‏قَوْمٍ فَتَوَضَّأَ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from his father, from Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī who said: I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ come to the water channel of a people and perform wuḍūʾ.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏كَانَ يُؤْتَى بِنَعْلَيْهِ وَهُوَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَيَلْبَسُهُمَا وَيَقُولُ إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Shuʿbah, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Abī Aws, from his grandfather: that his sandals would be brought to him while he was praying, and he would put them on, saying: 'I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ pray in his sandals.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَوَضَّأَ وَمَسَحَ عَلَى نَعْلَيْهِ ثُمَّ قَامَ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ ‏
en: Yaḥyā narrated to us, from Shuʿbah, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ narrated to us, from his father, from Aws ibn Abī Aws who said: I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ perform wuḍūʾ and wipe over his sandals, then stand for prayer.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏ ‏وَاسْتَوْكَفَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Abī Aws, from his grandfather: that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ prayed in his sandals and poured water over his hands three times.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَوْسًا ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏وَفْدِ ثَقِيفٍ ‏ ‏فَكُنَّا فِي ‏ ‏قُبَّةٍ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ مَنْ كَانَ فِيهَا غَيْرِي وَغَيْرَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏فَسَارَّهُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ اذْهَبْ فَاقْتُلْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَلَيْسَ يَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ قَالَ بَلَى وَلَكِنَّهُ يَقُولُهَا ‏ ‏تَعَوُّذًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رُدَّهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أُقَاتِلَ النَّاسَ حَتَّى يَقُولُوا لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَإِذَا قَالُوهَا حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيَّ دِمَاؤُهُمْ وَأَمْوَالُهُمْ إِلَّا بِحَقِّهَا ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ ‏ ‏لِشُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏أَلَيْسَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَلَيْسَ يَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏أَظُنُّهَا مَعَهَا وَمَا أَدْرِي ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān who said: I heard Aws say: I came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ with the delegation of Thaqīf, and we were in a tent. Everyone in it left except me and the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. A man came and whispered to him. He said: 'Go and kill him.' Then he said: 'Does he not testify that there is no god but Allāh?' The man said: 'Yes, but he only says it as a refuge.' He said: 'Bring him back.' Then he said: 'I have been commanded to fight people until they say lā ilāha illā Allāh. Once they say it, their blood and wealth are sacred to me except by right.' I said to Shuʿbah: 'Does the hadith not also say: "Does he not testify that there is no god but Allāh and that I am the Messenger of Allāh?"' Shuʿbah said: 'I think it is included, but I am not certain.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُمَرَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي هِلَالٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْجُمُعَةِ فَغَسَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ رَأْسَهُ وَاغْتَسَلَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏غَدَا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏ابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏دَنَا ‏ ‏فَاسْتَمَعَ وَأَنْصَتَ كَانَ لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ خَطَاهَا كَصِيَامِ سَنَةٍ وَقِيَامِ سَنَةٍ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj informed us, from ʿUmar ibn Muḥammad, from Saʿīd ibn Abī Hilāl, from Muḥammad ibn Saʿīd, from Aws ibn Aws, from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'On the day of Jumuʿah, if one of you washes his head and bathes, then goes early and arrives early, then draws near [to the imam] and listens and remains silent, he shall have for every step the reward of a year's fasting and prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الْجُعْفِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِنْ أَفْضَلِ أَيَّامِكُمْ يَوْمُ الْجُمُعَةِ فِيهِ خُلِقَ ‏ ‏آدَمُ ‏ ‏وَفِيهِ قُبِضَ وَفِيهِ النَّفْخَةُ وَفِيهِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْقَةُ ‏ ‏فَأَكْثِرُوا عَلَيَّ مِنْ الصَّلَاةِ فِيهِ فَإِنَّ صَلَاتَكُمْ مَعْرُوضَةٌ عَلَيَّ فَقَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَكَيْفَ تُعْرَضُ عَلَيْكَ صَلَاتُنَا وَقَدْ ‏ ‏أَرِمْتَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي وَقَدْ بَلِيتَ ‏ ‏قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ حَرَّمَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ أَنْ تَأْكُلَ أَجْسَادَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ ‏ ‏صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمْ ‏
en: Ḥusayn ibn ʿAlī al-Juʿfī narrated to us, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir, from Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī, from Aws ibn Aws who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Among the best of your days is Friday. On it Ādam was created, on it he passed away, on it is the Blast, and on it is the Swooning. So send abundant prayers upon me on it, for your prayers are presented to me.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how will our prayers be presented to you when you have decomposed?' — meaning, when you have decayed. He said: 'Allāh عز وجل has forbidden the earth from consuming the bodies of the Prophets, may the prayers of Allāh be upon them.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ بَكْرٍ السَّهْمِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَاتِمُ بْنُ أَبِي صَغِيرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَاهُ ‏ ‏أَوْسًا ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا لَقُعُودٌ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏الصُّفَّةِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَقُصُّ عَلَيْنَا وَيُذَكِّرُنَا إِذْ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏فَسَارَّهُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ اذْهَبُوا فَاقْتُلُوهُ قَالَ فَلَمَّا وَلَّى الرَّجُلُ دَعَاهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَيَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ قَالَ الرَّجُلُ نَعَمْ نَعَمْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ اذْهَبُوا فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُ فَإِنَّمَا ‏ ‏أُمِرْتُ أَنْ ‏ ‏أُقَاتِلَ النَّاسَ حَتَّى يَشْهَدُوا أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَإِذَا فَعَلُوا ذَلِكَ حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيَّ دِمَاؤُهُمْ وَأَمْوَالُهُمْ إِلَّا بِحَقِّهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو يُونُسَ حَاتِمُ بْنُ أَبِي صَغِيرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا لَقُعُودٌ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُنَا ‏ ‏وَيُوصِينَا إِذْ أَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مِثْلَهُ ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh ibn Bakr al-Sahmī narrated to us, Ḥātim ibn Abī Ṣaghīrah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim: that ʿAmr ibn Aws informed him, that his father Aws informed him, saying: We were sitting with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ at al-Ṣuffah while he was admonishing and reminding us, when a man came and whispered to him. He said: 'Go and kill him.' When the man turned to leave, the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ called him back and said: 'Does he testify that there is no god but Allāh?' The man said: 'Yes, yes, O Messenger of Allāh.' He said: 'Go and let him be, for I have been commanded to fight people until they testify that there is no god but Allāh. Once they do so, their blood and wealth are sacred to me except by right.' Muḥammad ibn ʿAbdillāh al-Anṣārī narrated to us, Abū Yūnus Ḥātim ibn Abī Ṣaghīrah narrated to us, al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim narrated to me: that ʿAmr ibn Aws informed him, from his father Aws who said: We were sitting with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ while he was speaking to us and advising us, when a man came — and he mentioned the like of it.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزُ بْنُ أَسَدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏يَوْمًا ‏ ‏تَوَضَّأَ فَمَسَحَ النَّعْلَيْنِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ أَتَمْسَحُ عَلَيْهِمَا فَقَالَ هَكَذَا رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَفْعَلُ ‏
en: Bahz ibn Asad narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed us, from Aws ibn Abī Aws who said: I saw my father one day perform wuḍūʾ and wipe over his sandals. I said to him: 'Do you wipe over them?' He said: 'This is how I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ [do it].'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الطَّائِفِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ حُذَيْفَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ فِي الْوَفْدِ الَّذِينَ أَتَوْا النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمُوا مِنْ ‏ ‏ثَقِيفٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏أَنْزَلَنَا فِي ‏ ‏قُبَّةٍ ‏ ‏لَهُ فَكَانَ ‏ ‏يَخْتَلِفُ ‏ ‏إِلَيْنَا بَيْنَ بُيُوتِهِ وَبَيْنَ الْمَسْجِدِ فَإِذَا صَلَّى الْعِشَاءَ الْآخِرَةَ انْصَرَفَ إِلَيْنَا وَلَا نَبْرَحُ حَتَّى يُحَدِّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَيَشْتَكِي ‏ ‏قُرَيْشًا ‏ ‏وَيَشْتَكِي أَهْلَ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏لَا سَوَاءَ كُنَّا ‏ ‏بِمَكَّةَ ‏ ‏مُسْتَذَلِّينَ وَمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فَلَمَّا خَرَجْنَا إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏كَانَتْ ‏ ‏سِجَالُ ‏ ‏الْحَرْبِ عَلَيْنَا وَلَنَا فَمَكَثَ عَنَّا لَيْلَةً لَمْ يَأْتِنَا حَتَّى طَالَ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْنَا بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ قَالَ قُلْنَا مَا أَمْكَثَكَ عَنَّا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ طَرَأَ عَلَيَّ حِزْبٌ مِنْ الْقُرْآنِ فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ لَا أَخْرُجَ حَتَّى أَقْضِيَهُ قَالَ فَسَأَلْنَا ‏ ‏أَصْحَابَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِينَ أَصْبَحْنَا قَالَ قُلْنَا كَيْفَ تُحَزِّبُونَ الْقُرْآنَ قَالُوا نُحَزِّبُهُ ثَلَاثَ سُوَرٍ وَخَمْسَ سُوَرٍ وَسَبْعَ سُوَرٍ وَتِسْعَ سُوَرٍ وَإِحْدَى عَشْرَةَ سُورَةً وَثَلَاثَ عَشْرَةَ سُورَةً وَحِزْبَ ‏ ‏الْمُفَصَّلِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏قَافْ حَتَّى يُخْتَمَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Ṭāʾifī narrated to us, from ʿUthmān ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Aws al-Thaqafī, from his grandfather Aws ibn Ḥudhayfah who said: I was among the delegation that came to the Prophet ﷺ from Thaqīf, from Banū Mālik. He lodged us in a tent and would go back and forth between his houses and the mosque. After praying the late ʿIshāʾ, he would come to us and we would not leave until he spoke with us, complaining about Quraysh and the people of Makkah. He would say: 'It was not equal — we were humiliated and oppressed in Makkah. When we went out to Madinah, the tides of war went back and forth between us and them.' One night he stayed away and did not come until it grew late after ʿIshāʾ. We said: 'What kept you from us, O Messenger of Allāh?' He said: 'A portion of the Qurʾān came due for me, and I did not want to leave until I completed it.' In the morning, we asked the companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ: 'How do you divide the Qurʾān into portions?' They said: 'We divide it into three sūrahs, five sūrahs, seven sūrahs, nine sūrahs, eleven sūrahs, thirteen sūrahs, and the ḥizb of al-Mufaṣṣal from Sūrat Qāf until the end.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Abī Aws, from his grandfather: that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ prayed in his sandals.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَرِيكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَوَضَّأَ وَمَسَحَ عَلَى نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, from Sharīk, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Aws ibn Abī Aws, from his father: that the Prophet ﷺ performed wuḍūʾ and wiped over his sandals.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏جَدُّهُ ‏ ‏أَوْسُ بْنُ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي وَيُومِئُ إِلَى نَعْلَيْهِ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَيَأْخُذُهُمَا فَيَنْتَعِلُهُمَا وَيُصَلِّي فِيهِمَا وَيَقُولُ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim narrated to us, from a man whose grandfather was Aws ibn Abī Aws: He would be praying and gesture for his sandals while in prayer, then take them and put them on and pray in them, saying: 'The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ used to pray in his sandals.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَوَضَّأَ ‏ ‏وَاسْتَوْكَفَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا أَيْ غَسَلَ كَفَّيْهِ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Abī Aws, from his grandfather Aws who said: I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ perform wuḍūʾ and pour water over his hands three times — meaning he washed his palms.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ بْنُ الْحَجَّاجِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَوَضَّأَ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَوْكَفَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏يَعْنِي غَسَلَ يَدَيْهِ ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ ‏ ‏لِشُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏أَدْخَلَهُمَا فِي الْإِنَاءِ أَوْ غَسَلَهُمَا خَارِجًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا أَدْرِي ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Shuʿbah ibn al-Ḥajjāj informed us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Abī Aws, from his grandfather Aws who said: I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ perform wuḍūʾ and pour water over his hands three times — meaning he washed his hands three times. I said to Shuʿbah: 'Did he put them in the vessel or wash them outside it?' He said: 'I do not know.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الْجُعْفِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بِهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ غَسَّلَ وَاغْتَسَلَ ‏ ‏وَغَدَا ‏ ‏وَابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏فَدَنَا ‏ ‏وَأَنْصَتَ وَلَمْ ‏ ‏يَلْغُ ‏ ‏كَانَ لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ كَأَجْرِ سَنَةٍ صِيَامِهَا وَقِيَامِهَا ‏
en: Ḥusayn ibn ʿAlī al-Juʿfī narrated to us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir narrated it, from Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī, from Aws ibn Aws who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever washes and bathes, goes early and arrives early, draws near [to the imam] and listens and does not engage in idle speech, he shall have for every step the reward of a year's fasting and prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَسَّانَ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ غَسَّلَ وَاغْتَسَلَ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَبَكَّرَ ‏ ‏وَابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏وَمَشَى وَلَمْ يَرْكَبْ ‏ ‏فَدَنَا ‏ ‏مِنْ الْإِمَامِ فَاسْتَمَعَ وَلَمْ ‏ ‏يَلْغُ ‏ ‏كَانَ لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ عَمَلُ سَنَةٍ أَجْرُ صِيَامِهَا وَقِيَامِهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَّانُ بْنُ عَطِيَّةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَوْسُ بْنُ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مِثْلَهُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏غَدَا ‏ ‏وَابْتَكَرَ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Ādam narrated to us, Ibn al-Mubārak narrated to us, from al-Awzāʿī, from Ḥassān ibn ʿAṭiyyah, from Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī, from Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī who said: I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ say: 'Whoever washes and bathes on the day of Jumuʿah, goes early and arrives early, walks and does not ride, draws near to the imam, listens, and does not engage in idle speech, he shall have for every step the deed of a year — the reward of its fasting and prayer.' Ibrāhīm ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, Ibn al-Mubārak narrated to us, from al-Awzāʿī, Ḥassān ibn ʿAṭiyyah narrated to us, Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī narrated to me, Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī narrated to me, he said: I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ — and he mentioned the like of it, except that he said: 'then went early and arrived early.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو الْأَشْعَثِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَوْسُ بْنُ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَذَكَرَ الْجُمُعَةَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ غَسَّلَ وَاغْتَسَلَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏غَدَا ‏ ‏وَابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏وَخَرَجَ يَمْشِي وَلَمْ يَرْكَبْ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏دَنَا ‏ ‏مِنْ الْإِمَامِ فَأَنْصَتَ وَلَمْ ‏ ‏يَلْغُ ‏ ‏كَانَ لَهُ كَأَجْرِ سَنَةٍ صِيَامِهَا وَقِيَامِهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَزَعَمَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ حَفِظَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ كَأَجْرِ سَنَةٍ صِيَامِهَا وَقِيَامِهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏وَلَمْ أَسْمَعْهُ يَقُولُ مَشَى وَلَمْ يَرْكَبْ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, ʿAlī ibn al-Mubārak informed us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir informed us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Dimashqī narrated to me, Abū al-Ashʿath narrated to me, Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī narrated to me, he said: I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ mention Friday and say: 'Whoever washes and bathes, then goes early and arrives early, and goes out walking without riding, then draws near to the imam and remains silent without engaging in idle speech, he shall have the reward of a year's fasting and prayer.' He said: Yaḥyā ibn al-Ḥārith claimed that he memorized from Abū al-Ashʿath that he said: 'For every step, the reward of a year's fasting and prayer.' Yaḥyā said: 'But I did not hear him say: walked and did not ride.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَكَمُ بْنُ نَافِعٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَاشِدِ بْنِ دَاوُدَ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ اغْتَسَلَ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَغَسَّلَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏ابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏وَغَدَا ‏ ‏إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ ثُمَّ جَلَسَ قَرِيبًا مِنْ الْإِمَامِ حَتَّى يُنْصِتَ كَانَ لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ خَطَاهَا عَمَلُ سَنَةٍ صِيَامُهَا وَقِيَامُهَا ‏
en: Al-Ḥakam ibn Nāfiʿ narrated to us, Ismāʿīl ibn ʿAyyāsh narrated to us, from Rāshid ibn Dāwūd al-Ṣanʿānī, from Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī, from Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī, from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Whoever bathes on the day of Jumuʿah and washes [his head], then goes early and arrives early to the mosque, and sits close to the imam so that he remains silent, he shall have for every step he takes the deed of a year — its fasting and its prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ جَدِّي ‏ ‏أَوْسٌ ‏ ‏أَحْيَانًا ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَيُشِيرُ إِلَيَّ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَأُعْطِيهِ نَعْلَيْهِ وَيَقُولُ رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from Ibn Aws who said: My grandfather Aws would sometimes be praying and gesture to me while in prayer, so I would give him his sandals. He would say: 'I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ pray in his sandals.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو أَحْمَدَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عِيسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَشْعَثِ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ غَسَّلَ وَاغْتَسَلَ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏غَدَا ‏ ‏فَابْتَكَرَ ‏ ‏وَجَلَسَ مِنْ الْإِمَامِ قَرِيبًا فَاسْتَمَعَ وَأَنْصَتَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ خَطْوَةٍ أَجْرُ سَنَةٍ صِيَامُهَا وَقِيَامُهَا ‏
en: Abū Aḥmad al-Zubayrī narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿĪsā, from Yaḥyā ibn al-Ḥārith, from Abū al-Ashʿath al-Ṣanʿānī, from Aws ibn Aws al-Thaqafī who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever washes and bathes, then goes early and arrives early, and sits close to the imam, and listens and remains silent, he shall have for every step the reward of a year's fasting and prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏فُلَانًا ‏ ‏أَوْسٌ ‏ ‏جَدُّهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏جَدِّي ‏ ‏يَقُولُ لِي وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ يُومِئُ إِلَيَّ نَاوِلْنِي النَّعْلَيْنِ فَأُنَاوِلُهُمَا إِيَّاهُ فَيَلْبَسُهُمَا وَيُصَلِّي فِيهِمَا وَيَقُولُ رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim narrated to us, he said: I heard so-and-so whose grandfather was Aws, who said: My grandfather would gesture to me while he was in prayer, [saying:] 'Hand me the sandals.' So I would hand them to him, and he would put them on and pray in them, saying: 'I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ pray in his sandals.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ حَفْصٍ ‏ ‏وَحُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏رَأَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَوَضَّأُ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَوْكَفَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ ‏ ‏اسْتَوْكَفَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏غَسَلَ يَدَيْهِ ثَلَاثًا ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn Ḥafṣ and Ḥusayn ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, they said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, he said: I heard ʿAmr ibn Aws narrating from his grandfather Aws ibn Abī Aws: that he saw the Prophet ﷺ performing wuḍūʾ and pouring water over his hands three times.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شَرِيكٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَوْسِ بْنِ أَبِي أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ مَعَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَلَى مَاءٍ مِنْ مِيَاهِ ‏ ‏الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏فَتَوَضَّأَ وَمَسَحَ عَلَى نَعْلَيْهِ فَقِيلَ لَهُ فَقَالَ مَا أَزِيدُكَ عَلَى مَا رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَصْنَعُ ‏
en: Al-Faḍl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, Sharīk narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Aws ibn Abī Aws who said: I was with my father at a watering place of the Arabs. He performed wuḍūʾ and wiped over his sandals. It was said to him [about this], and he said: 'I can only show you what I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ do.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الرُّؤْيَا عَلَى رِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ ‏ ‏تُعَبَّرْ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا ‏ ‏عُبِّرَتْ ‏ ‏وَقَعَتْ قَالَ وَالرُّؤْيَا جُزْءٌ مِنْ سِتَّةٍ وَأَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ قَالَ وَأَحْسِبُهُ قَالَ لَا يَقُصُّهَا إِلَّا عَلَى وَادٍّ أَوْ ذِي رَأْيٍ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed us, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'A dream hangs on the leg of a bird as long as it is not interpreted. Once interpreted, it falls [into reality].' He also said: 'A dream is one forty-sixth part of prophethood.' He said: And I think he said: 'One should only relate it to a loving friend or a person of sound judgment.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الرُّؤْيَا مُعَلَّقَةٌ بِرِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا صَاحِبُهَا فَإِذَا حَدَّثَ بِهَا وَقَعَتْ وَلَا تُحَدِّثُوا بِهَا إِلَّا عَالِمًا أَوْ نَاصِحًا أَوْ ‏ ‏لَبِيبًا ‏ ‏وَالرُّؤْيَا الصَّالِحَةُ جُزْءٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn, from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'A dream is suspended on the leg of a bird as long as its owner does not speak of it. Once he speaks of it, it falls [into reality]. Do not relate it except to a scholar, a sincere adviser, or a person of discernment. A righteous dream is one-fortieth part of prophethood.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ أَبِي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ الْحَجَّ وَلَا الْعُمْرَةَ وَلَا ‏ ‏الظَّعْنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ وَاعْتَمِرْ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from ʿAmr ibn Aws, from Abū Razīn al-ʿUqaylī: that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'My father is an old man who cannot perform Ḥajj, ʿUmrah, or travel.' He said: 'Perform Ḥajj and ʿUmrah on behalf of your father.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ أَبِي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ الْحَجَّ وَلَا الْعُمْرَةَ وَلَا ‏ ‏الظَّعْنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ وَاعْتَمِرْ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from ʿAmr ibn Aws, from Abū Razīn al-ʿUqaylī: that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'My father is an old man who cannot perform Ḥajj, ʿUmrah, or travel.' He said: 'Perform Ḥajj and ʿUmrah on behalf of your father.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَكُلُّنَا يَرَى اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَمَا آيَةُ ذَلِكَ فِي خَلْقِهِ قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَلَيْسَ كُلُّكُمْ يَرَى الْقَمَرَ مُخْلِيًا بِهِ قَالَ قُلْتُ بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَاللَّهُ أَعْظَمُ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah informed us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, will each of us see Allāh عز وجل on the Day of Resurrection? And what is the sign of that in His creation?' He said: 'O Abū Razīn, does not each of you see the moon clearly by himself?' I said: 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allāh.' He said: 'Then Allāh is greater.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ضَحِكَ رَبُّنَا مِنْ قُنُوطِ عَبْدِهِ وَقُرْبِ غَيْرِهِ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَوَيَضْحَكُ الرَّبُّ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ لَنْ نَعْدَمَ مِنْ رَبٍّ يَضْحَكُ خَيْرًا ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah informed us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Our Lord laughs at the despair of His servant when relief is near.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, does the Lord عز وجل laugh?' He said: 'Yes.' I said: 'We shall never lack goodness from a Lord who laughs.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَيْنَ كَانَ رَبُّنَا عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ خَلْقَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ فِي ‏ ‏عَمَاءٍ ‏ ‏مَا تَحْتَهُ هَوَاءٌ وَمَا فَوْقَهُ هَوَاءٌ ثُمَّ خَلَقَ عَرْشَهُ عَلَى الْمَاءِ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah informed us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, where was our Lord عز وجل before He created His creation?' He said: 'He was in a cloud, with nothing below it of air and nothing above it of air. Then He created His Throne upon the water.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَيْنَ أُمِّي قَالَ أُمُّكَ فِي النَّارِ قَالَ قُلْتُ فَأَيْنَ مَنْ مَضَى مِنْ أَهْلِكَ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَمَا ‏ ‏تَرْضَى أَنْ تَكُونَ أُمُّكَ مَعَ أُمِّي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏الصَّوَابُ ‏ ‏حُدُسٌ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, where is my mother?' He said: 'Your mother is in the Fire.' I said: 'Then where are those of your family who have passed?' He said: 'Would it not please you that your mother is with my mother?' My father [Aḥmad] said: The correct [pronunciation] is Ḥudus.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ أَبِي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ وَلَا ‏ ‏الظَّعْنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ وَاعْتَمِرْ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim informed me, he said: I heard ʿAmr ibn Aws narrating from Abū Razīn who said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, my father is an old man who cannot perform Ḥajj, ʿUmrah, or travel.' He said: 'Perform Ḥajj on behalf of your father and perform ʿUmrah.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ لَقِيطٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏رَفَعَهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رُؤْيَا الْمُؤْمِنِ جُزْءٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ ‏ ‏أَشُكُّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ رُؤْيَا الْمُؤْمِنِ عَلَى رِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ يُخْبِرْ بِهَا فَإِذَا أَخْبَرَ بِهَا وَقَعَتْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Sufyān informed us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Abū Razīn Laqīṭ, from his uncle — raised [to the Prophet ﷺ]. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The dream of a believer is one-fortieth part of prophethood.' — I am uncertain whether he said 'the dream of a believer' or 'the dream of a Muslim.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَكُلُّنَا يَرَى رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَمَا آيَةُ ذَلِكَ فِي خَلْقِهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَلَيْسَ كُلُّكُمْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى الْقَمَرِ مُخْلِيًا بِهِ قَالَ بَلَى قَالَ فَاللَّهُ أَعْظَمُ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى وَمَا آيَةُ ذَلِكَ فِي خَلْقِهِ قَالَ أَمَا مَرَرْتَ بِوَادِي أَهْلِكَ ‏ ‏مَحْلًا ‏ ‏قَالَ بَلَى قَالَ أَمَا مَرَرْتَ بِهِ يَهْتَزُّ خَضِرًا قَالَ قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَرْتَ بِهِ ‏ ‏مَحْلًا ‏ ‏قَالَ بَلَى قَالَ فَكَذَلِكَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى وَذَلِكَ آيَتُهُ فِي خَلْقِهِ ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed us, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudas, from his uncle Abū Razīn al-ʿUqaylī who said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, will each of us see his Lord عز وجل on the Day of Resurrection? And what is the sign of that in His creation?' The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Does not each of you look at the moon clearly by himself?' He said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Then Allāh is greater.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how does Allāh bring the dead to life, and what is the sign of that in His creation?' He said: 'Have you not passed by the valley of your people when it was barren?' He said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Then have you not passed by it when it was lush and green?' I said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Then have you not passed by it when it was barren [again]?' He said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Likewise Allāh brings the dead to life, and that is His sign in His creation.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَمَا مَرَرْتَ بِالْوَادِي ‏ ‏مُمْحِلًا ‏ ‏ثُمَّ تَمُرُّ بِهِ خَضِرًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَهُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ‏ ‏كَذَلِكَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how does Allāh bring the dead to life?' He said: 'Have you not passed by a valley that was barren, then passed by it when it was green?' — Shuʿbah said: He said it more than twice — 'Likewise Allāh brings the dead to life.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى قَالَ ‏ ‏أَمَا مَرَرْتَ بِأَرْضٍ مِنْ أَرْضِكَ مُجْدِبَةٍ ثُمَّ مَرَرْتَ بِهَا مُخْصَبَةً قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ كَذَلِكَ النُّشُورُ قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَمَا الْإِيمَانُ قَالَ أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَأَنَّ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدًا ‏ ‏عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَأَنْ يَكُونَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكَ مِمَّا سِوَاهُمَا وَأَنْ تُحْرَقَ بِالنَّارِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِاللَّهِ وَأَنْ تُحِبَّ غَيْرَ ذِي نَسَبٍ لَا تُحِبُّهُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَإِذَا كُنْتَ كَذَلِكَ فَقَدْ دَخَلَ حُبُّ الْإِيمَانِ فِي قَلْبِكَ كَمَا دَخَلَ حُبُّ الْمَاءِ لِلظَّمْآنِ فِي الْيَوْمِ ‏ ‏الْقَائِظِ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ لِي ‏ ‏بِأَنْ أَعْلَمَ أَنِّي مُؤْمِنٌ قَالَ مَا مِنْ أُمَّتِي ‏ ‏أَوْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ ‏ ‏عَبْدٌ يَعْمَلُ حَسَنَةً فَيَعْلَمُ أَنَّهَا حَسَنَةٌ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ جَازِيهِ بِهَا خَيْرًا وَلَا يَعْمَلُ سَيِّئَةً فَيَعْلَمُ أَنَّهَا سَيِّئَةٌ وَاسْتَغْفَرَ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مِنْهَا وَيَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ إِلَّا هُوَ إِلَّا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, ʿAbdullāh — meaning Ibn al-Mubārak — informed us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir informed us, from Sulaymān ibn Mūsā, from Abū Razīn al-ʿUqaylī who said: I came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how does Allāh bring the dead to life?' He said: 'Have you not passed by land of yours that was barren, then passed by it when it was fertile?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Likewise is the Resurrection.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what is faith?' He said: 'That you testify there is no god but Allāh alone, with no partner, and that Muḥammad is His slave and messenger; and that Allāh and His Messenger are more beloved to you than all else; and that being burned in fire is more beloved to you than associating partners with Allāh; and that you love someone unrelated by kinship, loving him only for the sake of Allāh عز وجل. When you are like that, the love of faith has entered your heart just as the love of water enters the thirsty man on a scorching day.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how can I know that I am a believer?' He said: 'There is no servant of my ummah — or this ummah — who does a good deed knowing it is good and that Allāh عز وجل will reward him for it, and who does not do a bad deed without knowing it is bad and seeking forgiveness from Allāh عز وجل, knowing that none forgives except Him, except that he is a believer.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏وَكِيعَ بْنَ حُدُسٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ رُؤْيَا الْمُسْلِمِ جُزْءٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ وَهِيَ عَلَى رِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا فَإِذَا حَدَّثَ بِهَا وَقَعَتْ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَظُنُّهُ قَالَ لَا يُحَدِّثُ بِهَا إِلَّا حَبِيبًا أَوْ ‏ ‏لَبِيبًا ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed me, he said: I heard Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus narrating from his uncle Abū Razīn: that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'The dream of a Muslim is one-fortieth part of prophethood. It hangs on the leg of a bird as long as he does not speak of it. Once he speaks of it, it falls [into reality].' He said: I think he said: 'One should relate it only to a beloved friend or a person of discernment.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَابْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَمَا مَرَرْتَ بِوَادٍ ‏ ‏مُمْحِلٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَرْتَ بِهِ خَصِيبًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ تَمُرُّ بِهِ خَضِرًا ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ كَذَلِكَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَى ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān and Ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, they said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how does Allāh bring the dead to life?' He said: 'Have you not passed by a valley that was barren, then passed by it when it was fertile?' — Ibn Jaʿfar said: 'then passed by it when it was green' — I said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Likewise Allāh brings the dead to life.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏وَبَهْزٌ ‏ ‏الْمَعْنَى ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏وَكِيعَ بْنَ حُدَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رُؤْيَا الْمُؤْمِنِ جُزْءٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ وَهِيَ عَلَى رِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا فَإِذَا حَدَّثَ بِهَا سَقَطَتْ ‏ ‏وَأَحْسِبُهُ قَالَ لَا يُحَدِّثُ بِهَا إِلَّا حَبِيبًا أَوْ ‏ ‏لَبِيبًا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī and Bahz narrated to us — with the same meaning — they said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ — Bahz said in his narration: Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed me — he said: I heard Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudas, from his uncle Abū Razīn who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'The dream of a believer is one-fortieth part of prophethood. It hangs on the leg of a bird as long as he does not speak of it. Once he speaks of it, it falls.' And I think he said: 'One should relate it only to a beloved friend or a person of discernment.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَبَهْزٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ عُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏أَكُلُّنَا يَرَى رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏كَيْفَ نَرَى رَبَّنَا ‏ ‏يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَمَا آيَةُ ذَلِكَ فِي خَلْقِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَلَيْسَ كُلُّكُمْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى الْقَمَرِ مُخْلِيًا بِهِ قَالَ قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ فَإِنَّهُ أَعْظَمُ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān and Bahz narrated to us, they said: Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn ʿUdus, from his uncle Abū Razīn — Bahz said: al-ʿUqaylī — who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh' — Bahz said: 'Will each of us see his Lord عز وجل?' — ʿAbd al-Raḥmān said: 'How will we see our Lord on the Day of Resurrection? And what is the sign of that in His creation?' He said: 'Does not each of you look at the moon clearly by himself?' I said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'Then He is greater.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏وَعَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ أَبِي شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَا يُطِيقُ الْحَجَّ وَلَا الْعُمْرَةَ وَلَا ‏ ‏الظَّعْنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ وَاعْتَمِرْ ‏
en: Bahz and ʿAffān narrated to us, they said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim informed me, he said: I heard ʿAmr ibn Aws who said: Abū Razīn said — ʿAffān said in his narration: from Abū Razīn — who said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, my father is an old man who can no longer perform Ḥajj, ʿUmrah, or travel.' He said: 'Perform Ḥajj on behalf of your father and perform ʿUmrah.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَيْنَ كَانَ رَبُّنَا عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ قَالَ فِي ‏ ‏عَمَاءٍ ‏ ‏مَا فَوْقَهُ هَوَاءٌ وَتَحْتَهُ هَوَاءٌ ثُمَّ خَلَقَ عَرْشَهُ عَلَى الْمَاءِ ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ informed me, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus, from his uncle Abū Razīn al-ʿUqaylī who said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, where was our Lord عز وجل before He created the heavens and the earth?' He said: 'In a cloud, with nothing above it of air and nothing below it of air. Then He created His Throne upon the water.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏وَحَسَنٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ ‏ ‏الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏ضَحِكَ رَبُّنَا مِنْ قُنُوطِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِهِ ‏ ‏وَقُرْبِ غَيْرِهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَوَيَضْحَكُ الرَّبُّ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ الْعَظِيمُ لَنْ نَعْدَمَ مِنْ رَبٍّ يَضْحَكُ خَيْرًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ لَنْ نَعْدَمَ مِنْ رَبٍّ يَضْحَكُ خَيْرًا ‏
en: Bahz and Ḥasan narrated to us, they said: Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudas, from his uncle Abū Razīn — Ḥasan said: al-ʿUqaylī — from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Our Lord laughs at the despair of His servant when relief is near.' Abū Razīn said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, does the Lord عز وجل, the Almighty, laugh?' He said: 'We shall never lack goodness from a Lord who laughs.' Ḥasan said in his narration: He said: 'Yes. We shall never lack goodness from a Lord who laughs.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏وَعَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدُسٍ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ وَهُوَ لَقِيطُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنَّا كُنَّا نَذْبَحُ فِي رَجَبٍ ذَبَائِحَ فَنَأْكُلُ مِنْهَا وَنُطْعِمُ مِنْهَا مَنْ جَاءَنَا قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏فَلَا أَدَعُهَا أَبَدًا ‏
en: Bahz and ʿAffān narrated to us, they said: Abū ʿAwānah narrated to us, Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ narrated to us, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus al-ʿUqaylī, from his uncle Abū Razīn — who is Laqīṭ ibn ʿĀmir — Abū Razīn informed me that he said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, we used to slaughter sacrifices in Rajab, eating from them and feeding whoever came to us.' The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'There is no harm in that.' Wakīʿ said: 'Then I shall never abandon it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ أَوْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ أَبِي أَدْرَكَ الْإِسْلَامَ وَهُوَ شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ الْحَجَّ وَلَا الْعُمْرَةَ وَلَا ‏ ‏الظَّعْنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حُجَّ عَنْ أَبِيكَ وَاعْتَمِرْ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Shuʿbah informed us, from al-Nuʿmān ibn Sālim, from ʿAmr ibn Aws, from his uncle Abū Razīn: that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'My father has reached Islam as an old man who cannot perform Ḥajj, ʿUmrah, or travel.' He said: 'Perform Ḥajj and ʿUmrah on behalf of your father.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ حَمَّادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدَسٍ أَبِي مُصْعَبَ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ وَهُوَ لَقِيطُ بْنُ عَامِرِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَفِقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنَّا كُنَّا نَذْبَحُ فِي رَجَبٍ ذَبَائِحَ فَنَأْكُلُ مِنْهَا وَنُطْعِمُ مِنْهَا ‏ ‏مَنْ جَاءَنَا قَالَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏لَا أَدَعُهَا أَبَدًا ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Ḥammād narrated to us, Abū ʿAwānah informed us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudas Abū Muṣʿab al-ʿUqaylī, from his uncle Abū Razīn — who is Laqīṭ ibn ʿĀmir ibn al-Muntafiq — Abū Razīn informed me that he said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, we used to slaughter sacrifices in Rajab, eating from them and feeding whoever came to us.' The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'There is no harm in that.' Wakīʿ said: 'Then I shall never abandon it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَكِيعِ بْنِ حُدُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رُؤْيَا الْمُسْلِمِ جُزْءٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ وَهِيَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي عَلَى رِجْلِ طَائِرٍ مَا لَمْ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا فَإِذَا حَدَّثَ بِهَا وَقَعَتْ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Yaʿlā ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Wakīʿ ibn Ḥudus, from his uncle Abū Razīn: that the Prophet of Allāh ﷺ said: 'The dream of a Muslim is one-fortieth part of prophethood. It hangs on the leg of a bird as long as he does not speak of it. Once he speaks of it, it falls [into reality].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَتَبَ إِلَيَّ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْكَ بِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ وَقَدْ عَرَضْتُهُ وَجَمَعْتُهُ عَلَى مَا كَتَبْتُ بِهِ إِلَيْكَ فَحَدِّثْ بِذَلِكَ عَنِّي ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ الْحِزَامِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ السَّمَعِيُّ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ الْقُبَائِيُّ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي عَمْرِو بْنِ عَوْفٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَلْهَمِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَاجِبِ بْنِ عَامِرِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَفِقِ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏لَقِيطِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏دَلْهَمٌ ‏ ‏وَحَدَّثَنِيهِ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْأَسْوَدُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏لَقِيطًا ‏ ‏خَرَجَ وَافِدًا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَمَعَهُ صَاحِبٌ لَهُ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏نَهِيكُ بْنُ عَاصِمِ بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَفِقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَقِيطٌ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجْتُ أَنَا وَصَاحِبِي حَتَّى قَدِمْنَا عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِانْسِلَاخِ ‏ ‏رَجَبٍ فَأَتَيْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَوَافَيْنَاهُ حِينَ انْصَرَفَ مِنْ صَلَاةِ ‏ ‏الْغَدَاةِ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ فِي النَّاسِ خَطِيبًا فَقَالَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ أَلَا إِنِّي قَدْ خَبَّأْتُ لَكُمْ صَوْتِي مُنْذُ أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ أَلَا لَأُسْمِعَنَّكُمْ أَلَا فَهَلْ مِنْ امْرِئٍ بَعَثَهُ قَوْمُهُ فَقَالُوا اعْلَمْ لَنَا مَا يَقُولُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَلَا ثُمَّ لَعَلَّهُ أَنْ يُلْهِيَهُ حَدِيثُ نَفْسِهِ أَوْ حَدِيثُ صَاحِبِهِ أَوْ يُلْهِيَهُ الضُّلَّالُ أَلَا إِنِّي مَسْئُولٌ هَلْ بَلَّغْتُ أَلَا اسْمَعُوا تَعِيشُوا أَلَا اجْلِسُوا أَلَا اجْلِسُوا قَالَ فَجَلَسَ النَّاسُ وَقُمْتُ أَنَا وَصَاحِبِي حَتَّى إِذَا فَرَغَ لَنَا فُؤَادُهُ وَبَصَرُهُ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا عِنْدَكَ مِنْ عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ فَضَحِكَ لَعَمْرُ اللَّهِ وَهَزَّ رَأْسَهُ وَعَلِمَ أَنِّي ‏ ‏أَبْتَغِي لِسَقَطِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏ضَنَّ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِمَفَاتِيحِ خَمْسٍ مِنْ الْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَا إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ قُلْتُ وَمَا هِيَ قَالَ عِلْمُ ‏ ‏الْمَنِيَّةِ ‏ ‏قَدْ عَلِمَ ‏ ‏مَنِيَّةَ ‏ ‏أَحَدِكُمْ وَلَا تَعْلَمُونَهُ وَعِلْمُ ‏ ‏الْمَنِيِّ ‏ ‏حِينَ يَكُونُ فِي الرَّحِمِ قَدْ عَلِمَهُ وَلَا تَعْلَمُونَ وَعَلِمَ مَا فِي غَدٍ وَمَا أَنْتَ طَاعِمٌ غَدًا وَلَا تَعْلَمُهُ وَعَلِمَ الْيَوْمَ ‏ ‏الْغَيْثَ ‏ ‏يُشْرِفُ عَلَيْكُمْ ‏ ‏آزِلِينَ ‏ ‏آدِلِينَ ‏ ‏مُشْفِقِينَ فَيَظَلُّ يَضْحَكُ قَدْ عَلِمَ أَنَّ غَيْرَكُمْ إِلَى قُرْبٍ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَقِيطٌ ‏ ‏لَنْ نَعْدَمَ مِنْ رَبٍّ يَضْحَكُ خَيْرًا وَعَلِمَ يَوْمَ السَّاعَةِ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّمْنَا مِمَّا تُعَلِّمُ النَّاسَ وَمَا تَعْلَمُ فَإِنَّا مِنْ قَبِيلٍ لَا يُصَدِّقُونَ تَصْدِيقَنَا أَحَدٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏مَذْحِجٍ ‏ ‏الَّتِي ‏ ‏تَرْبَأُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَخَثْعَمٍ ‏ ‏الَّتِي ‏ ‏تُوَالِينَا وَعَشِيرَتِنَا الَّتِي نَحْنُ مِنْهَا قَالَ تَلْبَثُونَ مَا لَبِثْتُمْ ثُمَّ يُتَوَفَّى نَبِيُّكُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ تَلْبَثُونَ مَا لَبِثْتُمْ ثُمَّ تُبْعَثُ الصَّائِحَةُ لَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مَا تَدَعُ عَلَى ظَهْرِهَا مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا مَاتَ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ الَّذِينَ مَعَ رَبِّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَأَصْبَحَ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يُطِيفُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَخَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ الْبِلَادُ فَأَرْسَلَ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ السَّمَاءَ ‏ ‏بِهَضْبٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ عِنْدِ الْعَرْشِ فَلَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مَا تَدَعُ عَلَى ظَهْرِهَا مِنْ ‏ ‏مَصْرَعِ ‏ ‏قَتِيلٍ وَلَا مَدْفِنِ مَيِّتٍ إِلَّا شَقَّتْ الْقَبْرَ عَنْهُ حَتَّى تَجْعَلَهُ مِنْ عِنْدِ رَأْسِهِ ‏ ‏فَيَسْتَوِي جَالِسًا فَيَقُولُ رَبُّكَ ‏ ‏مَهْيَمْ ‏ ‏لِمَا كَانَ فِيهِ يَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ أَمْسِ الْيَوْمَ وَلِعَهْدِهِ بِالْحَيَاةِ يَحْسَبُهُ حَدِيثًا بِأَهْلِهِ فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يَجْمَعُنَا بَعْدَ مَا تُمَزِّقُنَا الرِّيَاحُ ‏ ‏وَالْبِلَى وَالسِّبَاعُ قَالَ أُنَبِّئُكَ بِمِثْلِ ذَلِكَ فِي ‏ ‏آلَاءِ ‏ ‏اللَّهِ الْأَرْضُ أَشْرَفْتَ عَلَيْهَا وَهِيَ ‏ ‏مَدَرَةٌ ‏ ‏بَالِيَةٌ فَقُلْتَ لَا ‏ ‏تَحْيَا أَبَدًا ثُمَّ أَرْسَلَ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَلَيْهَا السَّمَاءَ فَلَمْ تَلْبَثْ عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا أَيَّامًا حَتَّى أَشْرَفْتَ عَلَيْهَا وَهِيَ ‏ ‏شَرْيَةٌ ‏ ‏وَاحِدَةٌ وَلَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ لَهُوَ أَقْدَرُ عَلَى أَنْ يَجْمَعَهُمْ مِنْ الْمَاءِ عَلَى أَنْ يَجْمَعَ نَبَاتَ الْأَرْضِ فَيَخْرُجُونَ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَصْوَاءِ ‏ ‏وَمِنْ ‏ ‏مَصَارِعِهِمْ ‏ ‏فَتَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْهِ وَيَنْظُرُ إِلَيْكُمْ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ نَحْنُ مِلْءُ الْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ شَخْصٌ وَاحِدٌ نَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ وَيَنْظُرُ إِلَيْنَا قَالَ أُنَبِّئُكَ بِمِثْلِ ذَلِكَ فِي ‏ ‏آلَاءِ ‏ ‏اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ الشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ آيَةٌ مِنْهُ صَغِيرَةٌ تَرَوْنَهُمَا وَيَرَيَانِكُمْ سَاعَةً وَاحِدَةً لَا ‏ ‏تُضَارُّونَ ‏ ‏فِي رُؤْيَتِهِمَا وَلَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ لَهُوَ أَقْدَرُ عَلَى أَنْ ‏ ‏يَرَاكُمْ وَتَرَوْنَهُ مِنْ أَنْ تَرَوْنَهُمَا وَيَرَيَانِكُمْ لَا ‏ ‏تُضَارُّونَ ‏ ‏فِي رُؤْيَتِهِمَا قُلْتُ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَا يَفْعَلُ بِنَا رَبُّنَا عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِذَا لَقِينَاهُ قَالَ تُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْهِ بَادِيَةٌ لَهُ صَفَحَاتُكُمْ لَا ‏ ‏يَخْفَى عَلَيْهِ مِنْكُمْ خَافِيَةٌ فَيَأْخُذُ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِيَدِهِ غَرْفَةً مِنْ الْمَاءِ ‏ ‏فَيَنْضَحُ ‏ ‏قَبِيلَكُمْ بِهَا فَلَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مَا تُخْطِئُ وَجْهَ أَحَدِكُمْ مِنْهَا قَطْرَةٌ فَأَمَّا الْمُسْلِمُ فَتَدَعُ وَجْهَهُ مِثْلَ ‏ ‏الرَّيْطَةِ ‏ ‏الْبَيْضَاءِ وَأَمَّا الْكَافِرُ ‏ ‏فَتَخْطِمُهُ ‏ ‏مِثْلَ ‏ ‏الْحَمِيمِ ‏ ‏الْأَسْوَدِ أَلَا ثُمَّ يَنْصَرِفُ نَبِيُّكُمْ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَيَفْتَرِقُ عَلَى أَثَرِهِ الصَّالِحُونَ فَيَسْلُكُونَ جِسْرًا مِنْ النَّارِ ‏ ‏فَيَطَأُ ‏ ‏أَحَدُكُمْ الْجَمْرَ فَيَقُولُ حَسِّ يَقُولُ رَبُّكَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَوَانُهُ أَلَا فَتَطَّلِعُونَ عَلَى حَوْضِ الرَّسُولِ عَلَى ‏ ‏أَظْمَأِ وَاللَّهِ ‏ ‏نَاهِلَةٍ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهَا قَطُّ مَا رَأَيْتُهَا فَلَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مَا يَبْسُطُ وَاحِدٌ مِنْكُمْ يَدَهُ إِلَّا وُضِعَ عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏قَدَحٌ ‏ ‏يُطَهِّرُهُ مِنْ الطَّوْفِ وَالْبَوْلِ وَالْأَذَى وَتُحْبَسُ الشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ وَلَا تَرَوْنَ مِنْهُمَا وَاحِدًا قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَبِمَا نُبْصِرُ قَالَ بِمِثْلِ بَصَرِكَ سَاعَتَكَ هَذِهِ وَذَلِكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ فِي يَوْمٍ أَشْرَقَتْ الْأَرْضُ وَاجَهَتْ بِهِ الْجِبَالَ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَبِمَا ‏ ‏نُجْزَى مِنْ سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَحَسَنَاتِنَا قَالَ الْحَسَنَةُ بِعَشْرِ أَمْثَالِهَا وَالسَّيِّئَةُ بِمِثْلِهَا إِلَّا أَنْ يَعْفُوَ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِمَّا الْجَنَّةُ إِمَّا النَّارُ قَالَ لَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ إِنَّ لِلنَّارِ لَسَبْعَةَ أَبْوَابٍ مَا مِنْهُنَّ بَابَانِ إِلَّا يَسِيرُ الرَّاكِبُ بَيْنَهُمَا سَبْعِينَ عَامًا وَإِنَّ لِلْجَنَّةِ لَثَمَانِيَةَ أَبْوَابٍ مَا مِنْهُمَا بَابَانِ إِلَّا يَسِيرُ الرَّاكِبُ بَيْنَهُمَا سَبْعِينَ عَامًا قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَعَلَى مَا نَطَّلِعُ مِنْ الْجَنَّةِ قَالَ عَلَى أَنْهَارٍ مِنْ عَسَلٍ مُصَفًّى وَأَنْهَارٍ مِنْ كَأْسٍ مَا بِهَا مِنْ صُدَاعٍ وَلَا نَدَامَةٍ وَأَنْهَارٍ مِنْ لَبَنٍ لَمْ يَتَغَيَّرْ طَعْمُهُ وَمَاءٍ غَيْرِ ‏ ‏آسِنٍ ‏ ‏وَبِفَاكِهَةٍ لَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مَا تَعْلَمُونَ وَخَيْرٌ مِنْ مِثْلِهِ مَعَهُ وَأَزْوَاجٌ مُطَهَّرَةٌ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَلَنَا فِيهَا أَزْوَاجٌ ‏ ‏أَوْ مِنْهُنَّ مُصْلِحَاتٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ الصَّالِحَاتُ لِلصَّالِحِينَ تَلَذُّونَهُنَّ مِثْلَ لَذَّاتِكُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَيَلْذَذْنَ بِكُمْ غَيْرَ أَنْ لَا تَوَالُدَ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَقِيطٌ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ أَقُضِيَ مَا نَحْنُ بَالِغُونَ وَمُنْتَهُونَ إِلَيْهِ فَلَمْ يُجِبْهُ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا أُبَايِعُكَ قَالَ فَبَسَطَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَدَهُ وَقَالَ عَلَى إِقَامِ الصَّلَاةِ وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ وَزِيَالِ الْمُشْرِكِ وَأَنْ لَا تُشْرِكَ بِاللَّهِ إِلَهًا غَيْرَهُ قُلْتُ وَإِنَّ لَنَا مَا بَيْنَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ فَقَبَضَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَدَهُ وَظَنَّ أَنِّي مُشْتَرِطٌ شَيْئًا لَا ‏ ‏يُعْطِينِيهِ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَحِلُّ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْنَا وَلَا ‏ ‏يَجْنِي امْرُؤٌ إِلَّا عَلَى نَفْسِهِ فَبَسَطَ يَدَهُ وَقَالَ ذَلِكَ لَكَ تَحِلُّ حَيْثُ شِئْتَ وَلَا ‏ ‏يَجْنِي عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا نَفْسُكَ قَالَ فَانْصَرَفْنَا عَنْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ هَذَيْنِ لَعَمْرُ إِلَهِكَ مِنْ أَتْقَى النَّاسِ فِي الْأُولَى وَالْآخِرَةِ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏كَعْبُ ابْنُ الْخُدْرِيَّةِ ‏ ‏أَحَدُ ‏ ‏بَنِي بَكْرِ بْنِ كِلَابٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ هُمْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏بَنُو الْمُنْتَفِقِ ‏ ‏أَهْلُ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَانْصَرَفْنَا وَأَقْبَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ هَلْ لِأَحَدٍ مِمَّنْ مَضَى مِنْ خَيْرٍ فِي جَاهِلِيَّتِهِمْ قَالَ قَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏عُرْضِ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ إِنَّ أَبَاكَ ‏ ‏الْمُنْتَفِقَ ‏ ‏لَفِي النَّارِ قَالَ فَلَكَأَنَّهُ وَقَعَ حَرٌّ بَيْنَ جِلْدِي وَوَجْهِي وَلَحْمِي مِمَّا قَالَ لِأَبِي عَلَى رُءُوسِ النَّاسِ فَهَمَمْتُ أَنْ أَقُولَ وَأَبُوكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا الْأُخْرَى أَجْهَلُ فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَأَهْلُكَ قَالَ وَأَهْلِي لَعَمْرُ اللَّهِ مَا أَتَيْتَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ قَبْرِ عَامِرِيٍّ أَوْ قُرَشِيٍّ مِنْ مُشْرِكٍ فَقُلْ أَرْسَلَنِي إِلَيْكَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏فَأُبَشِّرُكَ بِمَا يَسُوءُكَ تُجَرُّ عَلَى وَجْهِكَ وَبَطْنِكَ فِي النَّارِ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا فَعَلَ بِهِمْ ذَلِكَ وَقَدْ كَانُوا عَلَى عَمَلٍ لَا يُحْسِنُونَ إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ وَكَانُوا يَحْسِبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ مُصْلِحُونَ قَالَ ذَلِكَ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بَعَثَ فِي آخِرِ كُلِّ سَبْعِ أُمَمٍ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي نَبِيًّا فَمَنْ عَصَى نَبِيَّهُ كَانَ مِنْ الضَّالِّينَ وَمَنْ أَطَاعَ نَبِيَّهُ كَانَ مِنْ الْمُهْتَدِينَ ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh [ibn Aḥmad] said: Ibrāhīm ibn Ḥamzah ibn Muḥammad ibn Ḥamzah ibn Muṣʿab ibn al-Zubayr wrote to me: 'I have written this hadith to you, having verified and compiled it as I have written it to you, so narrate it from me.' He said: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Mughīrah al-Ḥizāmī narrated to me, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿAyyāsh al-Samaʿī al-Anṣārī al-Qubāʾī — of Banū ʿAmr ibn ʿAwf — narrated to me, from Dalham ibn al-Aswad ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Ḥājib ibn ʿĀmir ibn al-Muntafiq al-ʿUqaylī, from his father, from his uncle Laqīṭ ibn ʿĀmir. Dalham said: And my father al-Aswad also narrated it to me from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ: that Laqīṭ went out as a delegate to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ with a companion of his called Nahīk ibn ʿĀṣim ibn Mālik ibn al-Muntafiq. Laqīṭ said: My companion and I set out until we reached the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ at the end of Rajab. We came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and arrived just as he finished the dawn prayer. He stood among the people as a preacher and said: 'O people, I have been saving my voice for four days. Now let me make you hear! Is there any man whose people sent him saying: "Learn for us what the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ says," but then he let himself be distracted by his own thoughts, or his companion\'s talk, or by misguidance? I will be asked: "Did you convey?" Listen and you shall live! Sit down, sit down!' The people sat, and my companion and I stood until his attention and gaze were free for us. I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what do you know of the Unseen?' He laughed, by the life of Allāh, shook his head, and knew I was seeking to catch something from him. He said: 'Your Lord عز وجل has kept five keys of the Unseen that none knows but Allāh' — and he gestured with his hand. I said: 'What are they?' He said: 'Knowledge of death — He knows the death of each of you, though you do not. Knowledge of the seed when it is in the womb — He knows it, though you do not. Knowledge of what is in tomorrow — what you will eat tomorrow, though you do not know it. Knowledge of the day of rain — He looks upon you in distress and deprivation, worried, and He keeps laughing, knowing that relief is near.' Laqīṭ said: 'We shall never lack goodness from a Lord who laughs.' 'And knowledge of the Day of the Hour.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, teach us what you teach the people and what you know, for we are from a tribe that no one believes as we do — not Madhhij who tower over us, nor Khathʿam who are our allies, nor our own clan.' He said: 'You will remain for a time, then your Prophet ﷺ will pass away, then you will remain for a time, then the Scream will be sent — by the life of your God, it will leave nothing on its surface alive, and even the angels with your Lord عز وجل [will die]. Then your Lord عز وجل will traverse the earth, which will be empty of people. Then your Lord عز وجل will send rain from near the Throne, and by the life of your God, it will leave no place where a slain man fell or a dead man was buried without splitting open his grave, until he is seated upright from his head. Your Lord will say: "What is this?" — regarding what he was in. He will say: "O Lord, it was only yesterday" — for he thinks his life was recent. I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how will He gather us after the winds, decay, and wild beasts have scattered us?' He said: 'I will inform you of the like of that among Allāh\'s signs: you looked upon the earth when it was dead and decayed, and you said it would never live again. Then your Lord عز وجل sent rain upon it, and within days you looked upon it and it was one green expanse. By the life of your God, He is more able to gather them than water is able to gather the vegetation of the earth. They will emerge from their graves and their places of death, and you will look at Him and He will look at you.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, how can we — filling the earth — look at Him when He is one Being, and He look at us?' He said: 'I will inform you of the like of that among Allāh\'s عز وجل signs: the sun and moon are a small sign of His — you see them and they see you at the same moment without crowding in seeing them. By the life of your God, He is more able to see you and have you see Him than you are to see them while they see you without crowding.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what will our Lord عز وجل do with us when we meet Him?' He said: 'You will be presented before Him with your faces exposed — nothing of you will be hidden from Him. Your Lord عز وجل will take a handful of water in His hand and sprinkle it upon your faces. By the life of your God, not a drop will miss any of your faces. As for the Muslim, it will leave his face like a white silk garment. As for the disbeliever, it will brand his face like black tar. Then your Prophet ﷺ will depart, and the righteous will follow in his wake. They will cross a bridge over the Fire — one of you will step on an ember and say "Ouch!" and your Lord عز وجل will say: "Was that really it?" Then you will come upon the Pool of the Messenger — the thirstiest, by Allāh, that has ever come upon water as you have seen it. By the life of your God, none of you will stretch out his hand without a cup being placed upon it, purifying him of filth, urine, and impurity. The sun and moon will be withheld, and you will see neither of them.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, by what will we see?' He said: 'By the like of your sight at this very hour' — and that was before sunrise on a day when the earth was bright and facing the mountains. I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what will we be rewarded for our good and bad deeds?' He said: 'A good deed is ten-fold, and a bad deed its equal — unless He pardons.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, is it Paradise or the Fire?' He said: 'By the life of your God, the Fire has seven gates — between any two gates a rider would travel for seventy years. And Paradise has eight gates — between any two a rider would travel for seventy years.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what will we come upon in Paradise?' He said: 'Rivers of purified honey, rivers of wine causing no headache or regret, rivers of milk whose taste does not change, water that is not stale, and fruits — by the life of your God, such as you do not know, and better than the like of them besides, and purified spouses.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, will we have wives there, or will some of them be righteous?' He said: 'The righteous for the righteous — you will enjoy them as you enjoy in this world, and they will enjoy you, but there will be no childbearing.' Laqīṭ said: I said: 'Is that the utmost of what we will reach?' But the Prophet ﷺ did not answer him. I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, on what shall I pledge allegiance to you?' The Prophet ﷺ extended his hand and said: 'On establishing the prayer, giving the zakāh, separating from the polytheists, and not associating any god with Allāh besides Him.' I said: 'And shall we have what is between the east and the west?' The Prophet ﷺ withdrew his hand, thinking I was stipulating something he would not give me. I said: 'We will settle wherever we wish, and no person shall bear the crime of another.' He extended his hand and said: 'That is yours — you may settle wherever you wish, and none shall bear the crime of another upon you.' We departed, and he said: 'These two, by the life of your God, are among the most God-fearing of people in this world and the next.' Kaʿb ibn al-Khudriyyah — of Banū Bakr ibn Kilāb — said to him: 'Who are they, O Messenger of Allāh?' He said: 'Banū al-Muntafiq — they are worthy of that.' We departed, and I turned to him and said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, is there any good for anyone who has passed in their time of ignorance?' A man from among Quraysh said: 'By Allāh, your father al-Muntafiq is in the Fire.' He said: It was as if hot coals fell between my skin and flesh because of what he said about my father before the people. I wanted to say: 'And your father, O Messenger of Allāh?' But then the better judgment prevailed, so I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, and your family?' He said: 'And my family. By the life of Allāh, any grave of an ʿĀmirī or Qurashī polytheist that you pass, say: "Muḥammad has sent me to you to give you tidings of what will grieve you — you will be dragged on your face and belly in the Fire."' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, what brought that upon them when they were doing deeds they thought were good, and they thought they were doing right?' He said: 'That is because Allāh عز وجل sent a prophet to every seven nations, and whoever disobeyed his prophet was among the misguided, and whoever obeyed his prophet was among the rightly guided.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْحَجَّاجِ النَّاجِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْقَاهِرِ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏ابْنٌ لِكِنَانَةَ بْنِ عَبَّاسِ بْنِ مِرْدَاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَاهُ ‏ ‏الْعَبَّاسَ بْنَ مِرْدَاسٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏دَعَا عَشِيَّةَ ‏ ‏عَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏لِأُمَّتِهِ بِالْمَغْفِرَةِ وَالرَّحْمَةِ فَأَكْثَرَ الدُّعَاءَ فَأَجَابَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْ قَدْ فَعَلْتُ وَغَفَرْتُ لِأُمَّتِكَ إِلَّا مَنْ ظَلَمَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا فَقَالَ يَا رَبِّ إِنَّكَ قَادِرٌ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِلظَّالِمِ وَتُثِيبَ الْمَظْلُومَ خَيْرًا مِنْ مَظْلَمَتِهِ فَلَمْ يَكُنْ فِي تِلْكَ الْعَشِيَّةِ إِلَّا ذَا فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏كَانَ مِنْ الْغَدِ دَعَا ‏ ‏غَدَاةَ ‏ ‏الْمُزْدَلِفَةِ ‏ ‏فَعَادَ يَدْعُو لِأُمَّتِهِ فَلَمْ يَلْبَثْ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنْ تَبَسَّمَ فَقَالَ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِهِ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَأُمِّي ضَحِكْتَ فِي سَاعَةٍ لَمْ تَكُنْ تَضْحَكُ فِيهَا فَمَا أَضْحَكَكَ أَضْحَكَ اللَّهُ سِنَّكَ قَالَ تَبَسَّمْتُ مِنْ عَدُوِّ اللَّهِ إِبْلِيسَ حِينَ عَلِمَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَدْ اسْتَجَابَ لِي فِي أُمَّتِي وَغَفَرَ لِلظَّالِمِ أَهْوَى يَدْعُو ‏ ‏بِالثُّبُورِ ‏ ‏وَالْوَيْلِ ‏ ‏وَيَحْثُو ‏ ‏التُّرَابَ عَلَى رَأْسِهِ فَتَبَسَّمْتُ مِمَّا يَصْنَعُ ‏ ‏جَزَعُهُ ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh [ibn Aḥmad] said: Ibrāhīm ibn al-Ḥajjāj al-Nājī narrated to me, ʿAbd al-Qāhir ibn al-Sarī narrated to us, a son of Kinānah ibn ʿAbbās ibn Mirdās narrated to me, from his father, that his father al-ʿAbbās ibn Mirdās narrated to him: that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ supplicated on the evening of ʿArafah for his ummah, asking for forgiveness and mercy, and he supplicated at length. Allāh عز وجل answered him: 'I have done so and forgiven your ummah, except those among them who wrong one another.' He said: 'O Lord, You are able to forgive the wrongdoer and compensate the wronged one with better than his grievance.' But that evening there was only that. The next morning at Muzdalifah, he supplicated again for his ummah. It was not long before the Prophet ﷺ smiled. Some of his companions said: 'O Messenger of Allāh — may my father and mother be your ransom — you laughed at a time when you do not usually laugh. What made you laugh? May Allāh make you smile.' He said: 'I smiled at the enemy of Allāh, Iblīs, when he learned that Allāh عز وجل had answered my prayer for my ummah and forgiven the wrongdoer. He began wailing with destruction and woe, pouring dust on his head. I smiled at his anguish.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏وَزَكَرِيَّا ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الشَّعْبِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ مُضَرِّسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏بِجَمْعٍ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ جِئْتُكَ مِنْ ‏ ‏جَبَلَيْ طَيِّءٍ ‏ ‏أَتْعَبْتُ نَفْسِي ‏ ‏وَأَنْصَبْتُ رَاحِلَتِي وَاللَّهِ مَا تَرَكْتُ مِنْ جَبَلٍ إِلَّا وَقَفْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَهَلْ لِي مِنْ حَجٍّ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ شَهِدَ مَعَنَا هَذِهِ الصَّلَاةَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي صَلَاةَ الْفَجْرِ ‏ ‏بِجَمْعٍ ‏ ‏وَوَقَفَ مَعَنَا حَتَّى ‏ ‏نُفِيضَ ‏ ‏مِنْهُ وَقَدْ ‏ ‏أَفَاضَ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ ‏ ‏عَرَفَاتٍ ‏ ‏لَيْلًا أَوْ نَهَارًا فَقَدْ تَمَّ حَجُّهُ وَقَضَى ‏ ‏تَفَثَهُ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Khālid and Zakariyyā, from al-Shaʿbī, ʿUrwah ibn Muḍarris informed me, he said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ while he was at Jamʿ (Muzdalifah). I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, I came to you from the two mountains of Ṭayyiʾ — I exhausted myself and exhausted my mount. By Allāh, I did not leave a mountain without standing upon it. Do I have a valid Ḥajj?' He said: 'Whoever witnesses this prayer with us — meaning the Fajr prayer at Jamʿ — and stands with us until we depart from it, having already departed from ʿArafāt before that, by night or by day, his Ḥajj is complete and he has fulfilled his rites.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زَكَرِيَّا ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الشَّعْبِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ مُضَرِّسِ بْنِ أَوْسِ بْنِ حَارِثَةَ بْنِ لَامٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ حَجَّ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ يُدْرِكْ النَّاسَ إِلَّا لَيْلًا وَهُوَ ‏ ‏بِجَمْعٍ ‏ ‏فَانْطَلَقَ إِلَى ‏ ‏عَرَفَاتٍ ‏ ‏فَأَفَاضَ ‏ ‏مِنْهَا ثُمَّ رَجَعَ فَأَتَى ‏ ‏جَمْعًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَتْعَبْتُ نَفْسِي ‏ ‏وَأَنْصَبْتُ ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتِي ‏ ‏فَهَلْ لِي مِنْ حَجٍّ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ صَلَّى مَعَنَا صَلَاةَ ‏ ‏الْغَدَاةِ ‏ ‏بِجَمْعٍ ‏ ‏وَوَقَفَ مَعَنَا حَتَّى ‏ ‏نُفِيضَ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ ‏ ‏أَفَاضَ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ ‏ ‏عَرَفَاتٍ ‏ ‏لَيْلًا أَوْ نَهَارًا فَقَدْ تَمَّ حَجُّهُ وَقَضَى ‏ ‏تَفَثَهُ ‏
en: Abū Nuʿaym narrated to us, Zakariyyā narrated to us, from al-Shaʿbī, ʿUrwah ibn Muḍarris ibn Aws ibn Ḥārithah ibn Lām narrated to me: that he performed Ḥajj in the time of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ but did not catch up with the people until nightfall while at Jamʿ. He went to ʿArafāt and departed from it, then returned to Jamʿ and said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, I exhausted myself and exhausted my mount. Do I have a valid Ḥajj?' He said: 'Whoever prays the dawn prayer with us at Jamʿ and stands with us until we depart, having already departed from ʿArafāt before that, by night or by day, his Ḥajj is complete and he has fulfilled his rites.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أُخْبِرْتُ أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيَّ ‏ ‏و عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏فُلَانٍ ‏ ‏و عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَبْلُغْ ‏ ‏أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةَ كُلَّهَا ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏أَتَى أَهْلَهُ فَوَجَدَ ‏ ‏قَصْعَةَ ‏ ‏ثَرِيدٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏قَدِيدِ ‏ ‏الْأَضْحَى فَأَبَى أَنْ يَأْكُلَهُ فَأَتَى ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ بْنَ النُّعْمَانِ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَامَ فِي حَجٍّ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِنِّي كُنْتُ أَمَرْتُكُمْ أَنْ لَا تَأْكُلُوا الْأَضَاحِيَّ فَوْقَ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ لِتَسَعَكُمْ وَإِنِّي أُحِلُّهُ لَكُمْ فَكُلُوا مِنْهُ مَا شِئْتُمْ قَالَ وَلَا تَبِيعُوا لُحُومَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيِ ‏ ‏وَالْأَضَاحِيِّ فَكُلُوا وَتَصَدَّقُوا وَاسْتَمْتِعُوا بِجُلُودِهَا وَإِنْ أُطْعِمْتُمْ مِنْ لُحُومِهَا شَيْئًا فَكُلُوهُ إِنْ شِئْتُمْ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj informed us: I was told that Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī — and from Sulaymān ibn Mūsā, from so-and-so — and from Abū al-Zubayr, from Jābir ibn ʿAbdillāh — though Abū al-Zubayr did not convey this entire story — that Abū Qatādah came to his family and found a bowl of tharīd made from dried sacrificial meat. He refused to eat it, then went to Qatādah ibn al-Nuʿmān who informed him that the Prophet ﷺ stood during a Ḥajj and said: 'I had commanded you not to eat the sacrificial meat beyond three days, so that it would suffice for all of you. Now I permit it for you — eat of it as you wish. Do not sell the meat of the sacrificial offerings — eat, give charity, and make use of their skins. If you are given any of their meat, eat it if you wish.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏زُبَيْدٌ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيَّ ‏ ‏أَتَى أَهْلَهُ فَوَجَدَ ‏ ‏قَصْعَةً ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏قَدِيدِ ‏ ‏الْأَضْحَى فَأَبَى أَنْ يَأْكُلَهُ فَأَتَى ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ بْنَ النُّعْمَانِ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَامَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِنِّي كُنْتُ أَمَرْتُكُمْ أَنْ لَا تَأْكُلُوا الْأَضَاحِيَّ فَوْقَ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ لِتَسَعَكُمْ وَإِنِّي أُحِلُّهُ لَكُمْ فَكُلُوا مِنْهُ مَا شِئْتُمْ وَلَا تَبِيعُوا لُحُومَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيِ ‏ ‏وَالْأَضَاحِيِّ فَكُلُوا وَتَصَدَّقُوا وَاسْتَمْتِعُوا بِجُلُودِهَا وَلَا تَبِيعُوهَا وَإِنْ أُطْعِمْتُمْ مِنْ لَحْمِهَا فَكُلُوا إِنْ شِئْتُمْ ‏ ‏و قَالَ ‏ ‏فِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَالْآنَ فَكُلُوا وَاتَّجِرُوا وَادَّخِرُوا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏نَحْوَ حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏زُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏هَذَا عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏لَمْ يَبْلُغْهُ كُلُّ ذَلِكَ عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏
en: Ḥajjāj narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj narrated to me, Sulaymān ibn Mūsā said: Zubayd informed me that Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī came to his family and found a bowl of dried sacrificial meat. He refused to eat it, then went to Qatādah ibn al-Nuʿmān who informed him that the Prophet ﷺ stood and said: 'I had commanded you not to eat the sacrificial meat beyond three days so it would suffice for you. Now I permit it — eat of it as you wish. Do not sell the meat of sacrificial offerings — eat, give charity, and make use of their skins. Do not sell them. If you are given any of their meat, eat it if you wish.' And in this hadith from Abū Saʿīd, from the Prophet ﷺ: 'So now eat, trade, and store.' Ḥajjāj narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, Abū al-Zubayr informed me, from Jābir — similar to this hadith of Zubayd from Abū Saʿīd — but he did not convey all of it from the Prophet ﷺ.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زُهَيْرٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَرِيكٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي نَمِرٍ تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏وَعَمِّهِ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُلُوا لُحُومَ الْأَضَاحِيِّ وَادَّخِرُوا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿAmr narrated to us, Zuhayr — meaning Ibn Muḥammad — narrated to us, from Sharīk — meaning Ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Abī Namir Tamīm — from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Saʿīd al-Khudrī, from his father and his uncle Qatādah: that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Eat the sacrificial meat and store it.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏وَأَبِي ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ خَبَّابٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏بَنِي عَدِيِّ بْنِ النَّجَّارِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ نَهَانَا عَنْ أَنْ نَأْكُلَ لُحُومَ ‏ ‏نُسُكِنَا ‏ ‏فَوْقَ ثَلَاثٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَخَرَجْتُ فِي سَفَرٍ ثُمَّ قَدِمْتُ عَلَى أَهْلِي وَذَلِكَ بَعْدَ الْأَضْحَى بِأَيَّامٍ قَالَ فَأَتَتْنِي ‏ ‏صَاحِبَتِي ‏ ‏بِسَاقٍ قَدْ جَعَلَتْ فِيهِ ‏ ‏قَدِيدًا ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ لَهَا أَنَّى لَكِ هَذَا ‏ ‏الْقَدِيدُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ مِنْ ضَحَايَانَا قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهَا أَوَلَمْ يَنْهَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ أَنْ نَأْكُلَهَا فَوْقَ ثَلَاثٍ قَالَ فَقَالَتْ إِنَّهُ قَدْ رَخَّصَ لِلنَّاسِ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَلَمْ أُصَدِّقْهَا حَتَّى بَعَثْتُ إِلَى أَخِي ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ بَدْرِيًّا أَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَبَعَثَ إِلَيَّ أَنْ كُلْ طَعَامَكَ فَقَدْ صَدَقَتْ قَدْ ‏ ‏أَرْخَصَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ فِي ذَلِكَ ‏
en: Yaʿqūb narrated to us, my father narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, Muḥammad ibn ʿAlī ibn Ḥusayn ibn Jaʿfar narrated to me — and my father Isḥāq ibn Yasār — from ʿAbdullāh ibn Khabbāb, the freedman of Banū ʿAdī ibn al-Najjār, from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ had forbidden us from eating our sacrificial meat beyond three days. He said: I went on a journey, then returned to my family some days after al-Aḍḥā. My wife brought me a leg in which she had made dried meat. I said to her: 'Where did you get this dried meat?' She said: 'From our sacrifices.' I said: 'Did the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ not forbid us from eating it beyond three days?' She said: 'He gave the people permission after that.'
