en: Wahb ibn Jarīr narrated to us, he said: My father narrated to me, he said: I heard Yūnus narrating from al-Zuhrī, from Muslim ibn Yazīd, one of Banū Saʿd ibn Bakr, that he heard Abū Shurayḥ al-Khuzāʿī, then al-Kaʿbī — who was a Companion of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ — saying: The Messenger of Allāh permitted us on the day of the conquest to fight Banū Bakr until we took our vengeance from them while he was in Makkah. Then the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ ordered the swords sheathed. The next day, a group of us met a man from Hudhayl in the Sacred Precinct heading toward the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ to accept Islam. He had wronged them in the pre-Islamic era and they had been seeking him. They killed him, rushing before he could reach the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and gain safety. When this reached the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, he was intensely angry. By Allāh, I never saw him angrier. We rushed to Abū Bakr, ʿUmar, and ʿAlī رضي الله عنهم seeking their intercession, fearing we were doomed. When the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ prayed, he stood and praised Allāh عز وجل as He deserves, then said: 'As for what follows: Indeed Allāh عز وجل is the One who made Makkah sacred, not the people. He only permitted it to me for one hour of yesterday's day, and today it is sacred as Allāh عز وجل made it sacred the first time. The most transgressive of people against Allāh عز وجل are three: a man who kills in it, a man who kills other than his killer, and a man who seeks a pre-Islamic vendetta. By Allāh, I will pay the blood money for this man you killed.' So the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ paid his blood money.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْمَقْبُرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي شُرَيْحٍ الْخُزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَمَّا بَعَثَ ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏بَعْثَهُ يَغْزُو ‏ ‏ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏أَتَاهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو شُرَيْحٍ ‏ ‏فَكَلَّمَهُ وَأَخْبَرَهُ بِمَا سَمِعَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَى نَادِي قَوْمِهِ فَجَلَسَ فِيهِ فَقُمْتُ إِلَيْهِ فَجَلَسْتُ مَعَهُ فَحَدَّثَ قَوْمَهُ كَمَا حَدَّثَ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏مَا سَمِعَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَعَمَّا قَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ هَذَا إِنَّا كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِينَ افْتَتَحَ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا كَانَ الْغَدُ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْفَتْحِ ‏ ‏عَدَتْ ‏ ‏خُزَاعَةُ ‏ ‏عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏هُذَيْلٍ ‏ ‏فَقَتَلُوهُ وَهُوَ مُشْرِكٌ فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِينَا خَطِيبًا فَقَالَ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ‏ ‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ حَرَّمَ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فَهِيَ حَرَامٌ مِنْ حَرَامِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا يَحِلُّ لِامْرِئٍ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أَنْ ‏ ‏يَسْفِكَ ‏ ‏فِيهَا دَمًا وَلَا ‏ ‏يَعْضِدَ ‏ ‏بِهَا شَجَرًا لَمْ تَحْلِلْ لِأَحَدٍ كَانَ قَبْلِي وَلَا تَحِلُّ لِأَحَدٍ يَكُونُ بَعْدِي وَلَمْ تَحْلِلْ لِي إِلَّا هَذِهِ السَّاعَةَ غَضَبًا عَلَى أَهْلِهَا أَلَا ثُمَّ قَدْ رَجَعَتْ كَحُرْمَتِهَا بِالْأَمْسِ أَلَا فَلْيُبَلِّغْ الشَّاهِدُ مِنْكُمْ الْغَائِبَ فَمَنْ قَالَ لَكُمْ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ قَاتَلَ بِهَا فَقُولُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَدْ أَحَلَّهَا لِرَسُولِهِ وَلَمْ يُحْلِلْهَا لَكُمْ يَا مَعْشَرَ ‏ ‏خُزَاعَةَ ‏ ‏وَارْفَعُوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْ الْقَتْلِ فَقَدْ كَثُرَ أَنْ يَقَعَ لَئِنْ قَتَلْتُمْ قَتِيلًا ‏ ‏لَأَدِيَنَّهُ ‏ ‏فَمَنْ قُتِلَ بَعْدَ مَقَامِي هَذَا فَأَهْلُهُ بِخَيْرِ ‏ ‏النَّظَرَيْنِ ‏ ‏إِنْ شَاءُوا فَدَمُ قَاتِلِهِ وَإِنْ شَاءُوا ‏ ‏فَعَقْلُهُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏وَدَى ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الرَّجُلَ الَّذِي قَتَلَتْهُ ‏ ‏خُزَاعَةُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏لِأَبِي شُرَيْحٍ ‏ ‏انْصَرِفْ أَيُّهَا الشَّيْخُ فَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِحُرْمَتِهَا مِنْكَ إِنَّهَا لَا تَمْنَعُ ‏ ‏سَافِكَ ‏ ‏دَمٍ وَلَا ‏ ‏خَالِعَ ‏ ‏طَاعَةٍ وَلَا مَانِعَ جِزْيَةٍ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ قَدْ كُنْتُ شَاهِدًا وَكُنْتَ غَائِبًا وَقَدْ بَلَّغْتُ وَقَدْ أَمَرَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يُبَلِّغَ شَاهِدُنَا غَائِبَنَا وَقَدْ بَلَّغْتُكَ فَأَنْتَ وَشَأْنُكَ ‏
en: Yaʿqūb narrated to us, he said: My father narrated to us, from Ibn Isḥāq, he said: Saʿīd ibn Abī Saʿīd al-Maqburī narrated to me, from Abū Shurayḥ al-Khuzāʿī who said: When ʿAmr ibn Saʿīd sent an expedition to Makkah to fight against Ibn al-Zubayr, Abū Shurayḥ came to him and told him what he had heard from the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. Then he went to the gathering of his people, and I went and sat with him. He told his people what he had told ʿAmr ibn Saʿīd of what he had heard from the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and what ʿAmr ibn Saʿīd had said to him. He said: I told him: We were with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ when he conquered Makkah. The day after the conquest, Khuzāʿah attacked a man from Hudhayl and killed him while he was a polytheist. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ stood among us as a speaker and said: 'O people, Allāh عز وجل made Makkah sacred the day He created the heavens and the earth. It is sacred by the sanctity of Allāh تعالى until the Day of Resurrection. It is not lawful for any person who believes in Allāh and the Last Day to shed blood in it or cut down a tree in it. It was not permitted for anyone before me, and it will not be permitted for anyone after me. It was only permitted to me for this hour, out of anger against its people. Indeed, it has returned to its sanctity as it was yesterday. Let those present convey to those absent. Whoever tells you that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ fought in it, say: Allāh عز وجل permitted it for His Messenger but did not permit it for you. O people of Khuzāʿah, lift your hands from killing, for there has been too much of it. If you kill anyone, I will pay his blood money. Whoever is killed after this speech of mine, his family has the choice of the better of two options: either the blood of his killer or his blood money.' Then the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ paid the blood money for the man whom Khuzāʿah had killed. ʿAmr ibn Saʿīd said to Abū Shurayḥ: Go on your way, old man. We know more about its sanctity than you. It does not protect a blood-shedder, nor one who rebels against authority, nor one who withholds tribute. He said: I said to him: I was there and you were absent. I have conveyed the message. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ commanded that those who were present convey to those who were absent. I have conveyed it to you, and you and your affair.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَجَدْتُ فِي كِتَابِ أَبِي بِخَطِّ يَدِهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَأَكْبَرُ عِلْمِي أَنَّ أَبِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ زُرَيْعٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ اللَّيْثِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي شُرَيْحٍ الْخُزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَعْتَى ‏ ‏النَّاسِ عَلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مَنْ قَتَلَ غَيْرَ قَاتِلِهِ أَوْ طَلَبَ بِدَمِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْإِسْلَامِ أَوْ بَصَّرَ عَيْنَيْهِ فِي النَّوْمِ مَا لَمْ ‏ ‏تُبْصِرَا ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh said: I found in my father's book in his own handwriting: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbdillāh narrated to us — and I strongly believe my father narrated it to us — he said: Yazīd ibn Zurayʿ narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, he said: al-Zuhrī narrated to us, from ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yazīd al-Laythī, from Abū Shurayḥ al-Khuzāʿī, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Indeed, among the most transgressive of people against Allāh عز وجل is one who kills other than his killer, or one who seeks blood vengeance from the pre-Islamic era from the people of Islam, or one whose eyes claim to have seen in sleep what they did not see."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏فَيَّاضُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الرَّقِّيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ الْكِلَابِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْهَمْدَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَمَّا فَتَحَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏جَعَلَ أَهْلُ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏يَأْتُونَهُ بِصِبْيَانِهِمْ ‏ ‏فَيَمْسَحُ عَلَى رُءُوسِهِمْ وَيَدْعُو لَهُمْ فَجِيءَ بِي إِلَيْهِ وَإِنِّي مُطَيَّبٌ ‏ ‏بِالْخَلُوقِ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَمْسَحْ عَلَى رَأْسِي وَلَمْ يَمْنَعْهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا أَنَّ أُمِّي ‏ ‏خَلَّقَتْنِي ‏ ‏بِالْخَلُوقِ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ يَمَسَّنِي مِنْ أَجْلِ ‏ ‏الْخَلُوقِ ‏
en: Fayyāḍ ibn Muḥammad al-Raqqī narrated to us, from Jaʿfar ibn Burqān, from Thābit ibn al-Ḥajjāj al-Kilābī, from ʿAbdullāh al-Hamdānī, from al-Walīd ibn ʿUqbah who said: When the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ conquered Makkah, the people of Makkah began bringing their children to him. He would wipe their heads and pray for them. I was brought to him while I was perfumed with khalūq [a yellow-orange perfume], but he did not wipe my head. The only thing that prevented him from doing so was that my mother had perfumed me with khalūq, so he did not touch me because of the khalūq.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطِ بْنِ صَبِرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا اسْتَنْشَقْتَ فَبَالِغْ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ صَائِمًا ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Abū Hāshim, from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ ibn Ṣabirah, from his father who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "When you rinse your nose, be thorough, unless you are fasting."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هَاشِمٍ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطِ بْنِ صَبِرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ ‏ ‏فَخَلِّلْ ‏ ‏الْأَصَابِعَ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, he said: Sufyān narrated to us, from Abū Hāshim Ismāʿīl ibn Kathīr, from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ ibn Ṣabirah, from his father who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ, and he said: "When you perform wuḍūʾ, wash between the fingers."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هَاشِمٍ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطِ بْنِ صَبِرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَبَحَ لَنَا شَاةً وَقَالَ ‏ ‏لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ وَلَمْ يَقُلْ لَا ‏ ‏يَحْسَبَنَّ ‏ ‏إِنَّا إِنَّمَا ذَبَحْنَاهَا لَكَ وَلَكِنْ لَنَا غَنَمٌ فَإِذَا بَلَغَتْ مِائَةً ذَبَحْنَا شَاةً ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Abū Hāshim Ismāʿīl ibn Kathīr, from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ ibn Ṣabirah, from his father who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ and he slaughtered a sheep for us and said: "Do not think — and he did not say 'let him not think' — that we slaughtered it for you. Rather, we have sheep, and when they reach a hundred, we slaughter one."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطِ بْنِ صَبِرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ فَأَبْلِغْ فِي ‏ ‏الِاسْتِنْشَاقِ مَا لَمْ تَكُنْ صَائِمًا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ismāʿīl ibn Kathīr Abū Hāshim, from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ ibn Ṣabirah, from his father, from the Prophet ﷺ: "When you perform wuḍūʾ, be thorough in rinsing the nose, unless you are fasting."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ أَبُو هَاشِمٍ الْمَكِّيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ لَقِيطِ بْنِ صَبِرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏وَافِدِ ‏ ‏بَنِي الْمُنْتَفِقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏انْطَلَقْتُ أَنَا وَصَاحِبٌ لِي حَتَّى انْتَهَيْنَا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ نَجِدْهُ فَأَطْعَمَتْنَا ‏ ‏عَائِشَةُ ‏ ‏تَمْرًا ‏ ‏وَعَصَدَتْ ‏ ‏لَنَا ‏ ‏عَصِيدَةً ‏ ‏إِذْ جَاءَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَقَلَّعُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ هَلْ أُطْعِمْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قُلْنَا نَعَمْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَبَيْنَا نَحْنُ كَذَلِكَ ‏ ‏دَفَعَ ‏ ‏رَاعِي الْغَنَمِ فِي ‏ ‏الْمُرَاحِ ‏ ‏عَلَى يَدِهِ ‏ ‏سَخْلَةٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ هَلْ وَلَدَتْ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَاذْبَحْ لَنَا شَاةً ثُمَّ أَقْبِلْ عَلَيْنَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ وَلَمْ يَقُلْ لَا يَحْسَبَنَّ إِنَّا ذَبَحْنَا الشَّاةَ مِنْ أَجْلِكُمَا لَنَا غَنَمٌ مِائَةٌ لَا نُرِيدُ أَنْ تَزِيدَ عَلَيْهَا فَإِذَا وَلَّدَ الرَّاعِي ‏ ‏بَهْمَةً ‏ ‏أَمَرْنَاهُ بِذَبْحِ شَاةٍ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ الْوُضُوءِ قَالَ إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ ‏ ‏فَأَسْبِغْ ‏ ‏وَخَلِّلْ ‏ ‏الْأَصَابِعَ وَإِذَا اسْتَنْثَرْتَ فَأَبْلِغْ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ صَائِمًا قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ لِي امْرَأَةً فَذَكَرَ مِنْ طُولِ لِسَانِهَا وَإِيذَائِهَا فَقَالَ طَلِّقْهَا قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّهَا ذَاتُ صُحْبَةٍ وَوَلَدٍ قَالَ فَأَمْسِكْهَا وَأْمُرْهَا فَإِنْ يَكُ فِيهَا خَيْرٌ فَسَتَفْعَلْ وَلَا تَضْرِبْ ‏ ‏ظَعِينَتَكَ ‏ ‏ضَرْبَكَ أَمَتَكَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, he said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, he said: Ismāʿīl ibn Kathīr Abū Hāshim al-Makkī narrated to us, from ʿĀṣim ibn Laqīṭ ibn Ṣabirah, from his father — the delegate of Banū al-Muntafiq — who said: My companion and I set out until we reached the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, but we did not find him. ʿĀʾishah رضي الله عنها fed us dates and prepared ʿaṣīdah (porridge) for us. Then the Prophet ﷺ came walking briskly and said: 'Have you been given anything to eat?' We said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allāh.' While we were thus, the shepherd came to the pen with a newborn lamb in his hand. He said: 'Has she given birth?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Then slaughter a sheep for us.' Then he turned to us and said: 'Do not think — and he said "do not think" [second person], not "let him not think" [third person] — that we slaughtered the sheep for your sake. We have a hundred sheep and do not want them to increase beyond that. Whenever the shepherd delivers a lamb, we order him to slaughter a sheep.' Then he said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, tell me about wuḍūʾ.' He said: 'When you perform wuḍūʾ, be thorough and interlace the fingers. And when you clean your nose, be thorough — unless you are fasting.' He said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, I have a wife' — and he mentioned her sharp tongue and how she troubled him. He said: 'Divorce her.' He said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, she is a companion and has children.' He said: 'Then keep her and instruct her. If there is good in her, she will comply. And do not beat your wife as you would beat your slave-girl.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏وَيَزِيدُ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَعْنُ ‏ ‏الْمُؤْمِنِ كَقَتْلِهِ وَمَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ فِي الدُّنْيَا عُذِّبَ بِهِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلَيْسَ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏نَذْرٌ ‏ ‏فِيمَا لَا يَمْلِكُ وَمَنْ رَمَى مُؤْمِنًا بِكُفْرٍ فَهُوَ كَقَتْلِهِ وَمَنْ حَلَفَ ‏ ‏بِمِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, he said: Hishām narrated to us — and Yazīd said: Hishām informed us, he said: Yaḥyā narrated to me, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk, that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Cursing a believer is like killing him. Whoever kills himself with something in this world will be punished with it in the Hereafter. There is no binding vow upon a Muslim regarding what he does not own. Whoever accuses a believer of disbelief, it is like killing him. And whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying, he is as he said.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَالِدٍ الْحَذَّاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَلَفَ ‏ ‏بِمِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا مُتَعَمِّدًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Khālid al-Ḥadhdhāʾ, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk al-Anṣārī who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying and deliberately, he is as he said.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ عَذَّبَهُ اللَّهُ بِهِ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ ‏
en: And he said: 'Whoever kills himself with something, Allāh will punish him with it in the Fire of Hell.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَرْبٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏ثَابِتُ بْنُ الضَّحَّاكِ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ مِمَّنْ بَايَعَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَلَفَ عَلَى يَمِينٍ ‏ ‏بِمِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ وَمَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ عُذِّبَ بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَيْسَ عَلَى رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏نَذْرٌ ‏ ‏فِيمَا لَا يَمْلِكُ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Ṣamad narrated to us, Ḥarb narrated to us, Yaḥyā narrated to us, he said: Abū Qilābah narrated to me, he said: Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk al-Anṣārī — who was among those who pledged allegiance under the tree — narrated to me that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying, he is as he said. Whoever kills himself with something will be punished with it on the Day of Resurrection. And there is no binding vow upon a man regarding what he does not own.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ الشَّيْبَانِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَأَلْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَعْقِلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏الْمُزَارَعَةِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتُ بْنُ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَهَى عَنْ ‏ ‏الْمُزَارَعَةِ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd al-Wāḥid ibn Ziyād narrated to us, Sulaymān al-Shaybānī narrated to us, ʿAbdullāh ibn al-Sāʾib narrated to us, he said: I asked ʿAbdullāh ibn Maʿqil about sharecropping (muzāraʿah). He said: Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk narrated to us that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ forbade sharecropping.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبَانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَلَفَ عَلَى ‏ ‏مِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ وَلَيْسَ عَلَى رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏نَذْرٌ ‏ ‏فِيمَا لَا يَمْلِكُ وَمَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ فِي الدُّنْيَا عُذِّبَ بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, he said: Abān narrated to us, he said: Yaḥyā ibn Abī Kathīr narrated to us, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk al-Anṣārī, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying, he is as he said. There is no binding vow upon a man regarding what he does not own. And whoever kills himself with something in this world will be punished with it on the Day of Resurrection.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ الشَّجَرَةِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعْدُ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَلَفَ ‏ ‏بِمِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا مُتَعَمِّدًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ وَمَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ أَوْ ذَبَحَ ذَبَحَهُ اللَّهُ بِهِ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk — who was among the Companions of the Tree — then he said afterward: or from a man, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'Whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying and deliberately, he is as he said. And whoever kills himself with something — or slaughters himself — Allāh will slaughter him with it in the Fire of Hell.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏رَفَعَ الْحَدِيثَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ عُذِّبَ بِهِ وَمَنْ شَهِدَ عَلَى مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ قَالَ مُؤْمِنٍ ‏ ‏بِكُفْرٍ فَهُوَ كَقَتْلِهِ وَمَنْ ‏ ‏لَعَنَهُ ‏ ‏فَهُوَ كَقَتْلِهِ وَمَنْ حَلَفَ عَلَى ‏ ‏مِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏غَيْرِ الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا فَهُوَ كَمَا حَلَفَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk, who raised the hadith to the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Whoever kills himself with something will be punished with it. Whoever testifies against a Muslim — or he said: a believer — with disbelief, it is like killing him. And whoever curses him, it is like killing him. And whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying, he is as he swore.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتِ بْنِ الضَّحَّاكِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَلَفَ ‏ ‏بِمِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ كَاذِبًا مُتَعَمِّدًا فَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ وَمَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ عَذَّبَهُ اللَّهُ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn ʿĀṣim narrated to us, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, from Thābit ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever swears by a religion other than Islam while lying and deliberately, he is as he said. And whoever kills himself with something, Allāh will punish him with it in the Fire of Hell.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأُقِيمَتْ الصَّلَاةُ فَجَلَسْتُ فَلَمَّا صَلَّى قَالَ لِي أَلَسْتَ بِمُسْلِمٍ قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ فَمَا مَنَعَكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ مَعَ النَّاسِ قَالَ قُلْتُ صَلَّيْتُ فِي أَهْلِي قَالَ ‏ ‏فَصَلِّ مَعَ النَّاسِ وَلَوْ كُنْتَ قَدْ صَلَّيْتَ فِي أَهْلِكَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ مِحْجَنٍ الدِّيلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ صَلَّيْتُ فِي أَهْلِي فَأُقِيمَتْ الصَّلَاةُ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مَعْنَى حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, Zayd ibn Aslam narrated to us, from Busr ibn Miḥjan, from his father — and ʿAbd al-Razzāq said: Maʿmar informed us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Busr ibn Miḥjan, from his father — who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ and the iqāmah was called for prayer, but I sat down. When he finished praying, he said to me: 'Are you not a Muslim?' I said: 'Indeed I am.' He said: 'Then what prevented you from praying with the people?' I said: 'I had already prayed at home.' He said: 'Pray with the people even if you have already prayed at home.' Abū Nuʿaym narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Busr ibn Miḥjan al-Dīlī, from his father who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ having already prayed at home, and the iqāmah was called — and he mentioned the same meaning as the hadith of ʿAbd al-Raḥmān.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏: ‏ ‏مَالِكٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي الدِّيلِ ‏ ‏يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏بُسْرُ بْنُ مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ كَانَ فِي مَجْلِسٍ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأُذِّنَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّى ثُمَّ رَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَمِحْجَنٌ ‏ ‏فِي مَجْلِسِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا مَنَعَكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ مَعَ النَّاسِ أَلَسْتَ بِرَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَلَكِنِّي كُنْتُ قَدْ صَلَّيْتُ فِي أَهْلِي فَقَالَ لَهُ إِذَا ‏ ‏جِئْتَ فَصَلِّ مَعَ النَّاسِ وَإِنْ كُنْتَ قَدْ صَلَّيْتَ ‏
en: He said: I read to ʿAbd al-Raḥmān: Mālik, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from a man of Banū al-Dīl called Busr ibn Miḥjan, from his father Miḥjan, that he was in a gathering with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ when the adhān was called for prayer. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ rose and prayed, then returned, and Miḥjan was still in his seat. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said to him: 'What prevented you from praying with the people? Are you not a Muslim man?' He said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allāh, but I had already prayed at home.' He said to him: 'When you come, pray with the people even if you have already prayed.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏صَلَّى خَلْفَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَسَمِعَهُ ‏ ‏يَقْرَأُ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ ‏ ‏ق وَالْقُرْآنِ الْمَجِيدِ ‏ ‏وَيس وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ ‏
en: Yūnus narrated to us, Abū ʿAwānah narrated to us, from Simāk ibn Ḥarb, from a man from the people of Madīnah, that he prayed behind the Prophet ﷺ and heard him reciting in the Fajr prayer Sūrah Qāf — 'By the Glorious Qurʾān' — and Sūrah Yā-Sīn — 'By the Wise Qurʾān.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ ثَوْبَانَ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثٌ حَقٌّ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ الْغُسْلُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَالسِّوَاكُ وَيَمَسُّ مِنْ طِيبٍ إِنْ وَجَدَ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Saʿd ibn Ibrāhīm, he said: I heard Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Thawbān narrating, from a man of the Anṣār, from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'Three things are obligatory upon every Muslim on Friday: bathing, using the siwāk (tooth-stick), and applying perfume if he can find it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ ثَوْبَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَقٌّ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ يَغْتَسِلُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ يَتَسَوَّكُ وَيَمَسُّ مِنْ طِيبٍ إِنْ كَانَ لِأَهْلِهِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Saʿd ibn Ibrāhīm, from Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Thawbān, from a man of the Anṣār among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'It is a duty upon every Muslim to bathe on Friday, use the siwāk, and apply perfume if his family has it.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَتْنِي ‏ ‏أُمُّ كُلْثُومٍ ابْنَةُ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَتَيْتُهَا ‏ ‏بِصَدَقَةٍ ‏ ‏كَانَ أُمِرَ بِهَا قَالَتْ أَحَدُ رَبَائِبِنَا فَإِنَّ ‏ ‏مَيْمُونَ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏مَرَّ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا ‏ ‏مَيْمُونُ ‏ ‏أَوْ يَا ‏ ‏مِهْرَانُ ‏ ‏إِنَّا أَهْلُ بَيْتٍ نُهِينَا عَنْ ‏ ‏الصَّدَقَةِ ‏ ‏وَإِنَّ ‏ ‏مَوَالِيَنَا ‏ ‏مِنْ أَنْفُسِنَا وَلَا نَأْكُلُ ‏ ‏الصَّدَقَةَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from ʿAṭāʾ ibn al-Sāʾib, he said: Umm Kulthūm bint ʿAlī narrated to me. He said: I came to her with a charitable donation that had been directed. She said: 'One of our step-children, for Maymūn — or Mihrān — the freed slave of the Prophet ﷺ informed me that he passed by the Prophet ﷺ who said to him: "O Maymūn — or O Mihrān — we are a household that has been forbidden from accepting charity. And our freed slaves are of us, and we do not eat charity."'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِشَامٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَرْقَمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ مِنْ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ يَؤُمُّهُمْ وَيُؤَذِّنُ وَيُقِيمُ فَأَقَامَ يَوْمًا الصَّلَاةَ فَقَالَ لِيُصَلِّ بِكُمْ رَجُلٌ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَرَادَ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَذْهَبَ إِلَى الْخَلَاءِ وَأُقِيمَتْ الصَّلَاةُ فَلْيَذْهَبْ إِلَى الْخَلَاءِ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Hishām, he said: My father narrated to me, from ʿAbdullāh ibn Arqam, that he went out from Makkah — and he used to lead them in prayer and call the adhān and iqāmah. One day he called the iqāmah then said: 'Let one of you lead the prayer, for I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ say: "If one of you needs to relieve himself and the prayer has been called, let him go relieve himself first."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَقْرَمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏كَانَ مَعَ أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏بِالْقَاعِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏نَمِرَةَ ‏ ‏فَمَرَّ بِنَا ‏ ‏رَكْبٌ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ أَبِي يَا بُنَيَّ كُنْ فِي ‏ ‏بَهْمِكَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى آتِيَ هَؤُلَاءِ الْقَوْمَ فَأُسَائِلَهُمْ ‏ ‏فَدَنَا ‏ ‏وَدَنَوْتُ ‏ ‏فَكُنْتُ أَنْظُرُ إِلَى ‏ ‏عُفْرَتَيْ ‏ ‏إِبْطَيْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, he said: Dāwūd ibn Qays narrated to us, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Aqram, he said: My father narrated to me that he was with his father at al-Qāʿ near Namirah. A caravan passed by us, and my father said: 'O my son, stay with your young animals until I go meet those people.' He drew near and I drew near, and I could see the whiteness of the armpits of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ while he was prostrating.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَقْرَمَ الْخُزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي ‏ ‏أَقْرَمَ ‏ ‏بِالْقَاعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَمَرَّ بِنَا ‏ ‏رَكْبٌ ‏ ‏فَأَنَاخُوا ‏ ‏بِنَاحِيَةِ الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لِي أَبِي أَيْ بُنَيَّ كُنْ فِي ‏ ‏بَهْمِكَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى آتِيَ هَؤُلَاءِ الْقَوْمَ وَأُسَائِلَهُمْ قَالَ فَخَرَجَ وَخَرَجْتُ فِي ‏ ‏أَثَرِهِ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَحَضَرَتْ الصَّلَاةُ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّيْتُ مَعَهُ فَكُنْتُ أَنْظُرُ إِلَى ‏ ‏عُفْرَتَيْ ‏ ‏إِبْطَيْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كُلَّمَا سَجَدَ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, he said: Dāwūd ibn Qays narrated to us, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Aqram al-Khuzāʿī, from his father who said: I was with my father Aqram at al-Qāʿ. A caravan passed by us and dismounted by the side of the road. My father said to me: 'O my son, stay with your young animals until I go meet those people.' He went out and I went out following him, and there was the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. The time for prayer came, and I prayed with him. I could see the whiteness of the armpits of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ every time he prostrated.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَقْرَمَ الْخُزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ كَانَ مَعَ أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏بِالْقَاعِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏نَمِرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَمَرَّ بِنَا ‏ ‏رَكْبٌ ‏ ‏فَأَنَاخُوا ‏ ‏بِنَاحِيَةِ الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ أَبِي أَيْ بُنَيَّ كُنْ فِي ‏ ‏بَهْمِكَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى آتِيَ هَؤُلَاءِ ‏ ‏الرَّكْبَ ‏ ‏فَأُسَائِلَهُمْ قَالَ ‏ ‏دَنَا ‏ ‏مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏وَدَنَوْتُ ‏ ‏مِنْهُ وَأُقِيمَتْ الصَّلَاةُ فَإِذَا فِيهِمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّيْتُ مَعَهُمْ وَكَأَنِّي أَنْظُرُ إِلَى ‏ ‏عُفْرَتَيْ ‏ ‏إِبْطَيْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا سَجَدَ ‏
en: Abū Nuʿaym narrated to us, Dāwūd — meaning Ibn Qays — narrated to us, he said: ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Aqram al-Khuzāʿī narrated to me, he said: My father narrated to me that he was with his father at al-Qāʿ near Namirah. A caravan passed by us and dismounted by the side of the road. My father said: 'O my son, stay with your young animals until I go meet those riders.' He drew near to them and I drew near to him. The iqāmah was called, and among them was the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. I prayed with them, and it was as if I could see the whiteness of the armpits of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ when he prostrated.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي الْهَيْثَمِ الْعَطَّارُ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّةً ‏ ‏سَمِعَهُ مِنْ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمَّانِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ ‏ ‏وَمَسَحَ عَلَى رَأْسِي ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, he said: Yaḥyā ibn Abī al-Haytham al-ʿAṭṭār narrated to us, he said: I heard Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām — and he said on another occasion: he heard from Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām — who said: 'The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ named me Yūsuf and wiped over my head.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مِسْعَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَضْرِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمَّانِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Misʿar narrated to us, from Naḍr ibn Qays, he said: I heard Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām saying: 'The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ named me Yūsuf.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْمُنْكَدِرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِرَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏وَامْرَأَتِهِ اعْتَمِرَا فِي رَمَضَانَ فَإِنَّ ‏ ‏عُمْرَةً فِي رَمَضَانَ لَكُمَا كَحَجَّةٍ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏مَرَّةً وَلَمْ يَقُلْ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏ابْنَ الْمُنْكَدِرِ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ عُمْرَةً فِيهِ كَحَجَّةٍ ‏
en: Sufyān ibn ʿUyaynah narrated to us, he said: Ibn al-Munkadir narrated to us, he said: I heard Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām saying: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said to a man from the Anṣār and his wife: 'Perform ʿUmrah in Ramaḍān, for an ʿUmrah in Ramaḍān is like a Ḥajj for you.' Sufyān said on another occasion — without saying 'narrated to me,' meaning Ibn al-Munkadir — 'an ʿUmrah in it is like a Ḥajj.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو أَحْمَدَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي الْهَيْثَمِ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏أَجْلَسَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَجْرِهِ وَمَسَحَ عَلَى رَأْسِي وَسَمَّانِي ‏ ‏يُوسُفَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏سَلَّامُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مِسْكِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شَهْرُ بْنُ حَوْشَبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يُوسُفَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ ‏ ‏وَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ الْمَارَّ ‏
en: Abū Aḥmad al-Zubayrī narrated to us, Yaḥyā ibn Abī al-Haytham narrated to us, he said: I heard Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām saying: 'The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ seated me in his lap, wiped over my head, and named me Yūsuf.' Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Sallām ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Miskīn informed us, he said: Shahr ibn Ḥawshab narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Yūsuf ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Salām — and he mentioned the preceding hadith.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ ‏ ‏أَرِقَّاءَكُمْ ‏ ‏أَرِقَّاءَكُمْ ‏ ‏أَرِقَّاءَكُمْ ‏ ‏أَطْعِمُوهُمْ مِمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ وَاكْسُوهُمْ مِمَّا تَلْبَسُونَ فَإِنْ جَاءُوا بِذَنْبٍ لَا تُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَغْفِرُوهُ فَبِيعُوا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ وَلَا تُعَذِّبُوهُمْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, he said: Sufyān narrated to us, from ʿĀṣim — meaning Ibn ʿUbaydillāh — from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd, from his father, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: 'Your slaves! Your slaves! Your slaves! Feed them from what you eat and clothe them from what you wear. If they commit a sin that you do not wish to forgive, then sell the servants of Allāh, but do not torture them.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ الْمَخْزُومِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اسْتَسْلَفَ ‏ ‏مِنْهُ حِينَ غَزَا ‏ ‏حُنَيْنًا ‏ ‏ثَلَاثِينَ ‏ ‏أَوْ أَرْبَعِينَ ‏ ‏أَلْفًا فَلَمَّا انْصَرَفَ ‏ ‏قَضَاهَا ‏ ‏إِيَّاهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي أَهْلِكَ وَمَالِكَ إِنَّمَا جَزَاءُ ‏ ‏السَّلَفِ ‏ ‏الْوَفَاءُ وَالْحَمْدُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Ismāʿīl ibn Ibrāhīm ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Abī Rabīʿah al-Makhzūmī narrated to us, from his father, from his grandfather, that the Prophet ﷺ borrowed from him when he went on the expedition to Ḥunayn — thirty or forty thousand (dirhams). When he returned, he repaid it to him, then said: 'May Allāh bless you in your family and your wealth. The reward of a loan is repayment and praise.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي أَسَدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ سَأَلَ وَلَهُ ‏ ‏أُوقِيَّةٌ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏عَدْلُهَا ‏ ‏فَقَدْ سَأَلَ ‏ ‏إِلْحَافًا ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yasār, from a man of Banū Asad who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'Whoever begs while possessing an ūqiyyah (forty dirhams) or its equivalent has begged importunately.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَفْضَلُ الْكَلَامِ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, he said: al-Aʿmash narrated to us, from Abū Ṣāliḥ, from some of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'The best speech is: SubḥānAllāh (Glory be to Allāh), Alḥamdulillāh (Praise be to Allāh), Lā ilāha illAllāh (There is no deity but Allāh), and Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest).'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏وَحَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏غُنْدَرٌ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ رَبِّهِ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏مَنْ ‏ ‏رَأَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عِنْدَ ‏ ‏أَحْجَارِ الزَّيْتِ ‏ ‏يَدْعُو بِكَفَّيْهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏وَرَفَعَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏كَفَّيْهِ وَبَسَطَهُمَا ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, he said: Shuʿbah narrated to us — and Ḥajjāj said: Shuʿbah informed us — from ʿAbd Rabbihi ibn Saʿīd — and Ghundar said: ʿAbd Rabbihi ibn Saʿīd — from Muḥammad ibn Ibrāhīm, he said: One who had seen the Prophet ﷺ informed me — at Aḥjār al-Zayt (the Stones of Oil) — that he was supplicating with his palms. Ḥajjāj said: Shuʿbah raised his palms and spread them open.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَتِيكٍ ‏ ‏أَحَدِ ‏ ‏بَنِي سَلِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَتِيكٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ خَرَجَ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ مُجَاهِدًا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ثُمَّ قَالَ بِأَصَابِعِهِ هَؤُلَاءِ الثَّلَاثِ الْوُسْطَى وَالسَّبَّابَةِ وَالْإِبْهَامِ فَجَمَعَهُنَّ وَقَالَ وَأَيْنَ الْمُجَاهِدُونَ ‏ ‏فَخَرَّ ‏ ‏عَنْ دَابَّتِهِ ‏ ‏فَمَاتَ ‏ ‏فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ تَعَالَى أَوْ ‏ ‏لَدَغَتْهُ ‏ ‏دَابَّةٌ فَمَاتَ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ أَوْ مَاتَ ‏ ‏حَتْفَ ‏ ‏أَنْفِهِ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَاللَّهِ إِنَّهَا لَكَلِمَةٌ مَا سَمِعْتُهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَمَاتَ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ تَعَالَى وَمَنْ مَاتَ ‏ ‏قَعْصًا ‏ ‏فَقَدْ اسْتَوْجَبَ ‏ ‏الْمَآبَ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq informed us, from Muḥammad ibn Ibrāhīm ibn al-Ḥārith, from Muḥammad ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿAtīk — one of Banū Salimah — from his father ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿAtīk who said: I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ say: 'Whoever leaves his house as a fighter in the way of Allāh عز وجل' — then he gestured with these three fingers: the middle, the index, and the thumb, gathering them together, and said — 'and where are the true fighters? If he falls from his mount and dies, his reward is guaranteed by Allāh. Or if a creature stings him and he dies, his reward is guaranteed by Allāh. Or if he dies a natural death, his reward is guaranteed by Allāh عز وجل.' By Allāh, it is a word I never heard from any Arab before the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ: 'he dies, and his reward is guaranteed by Allāh.' 'And whoever dies killed in battle has earned the final return.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ بِلَالٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَاسٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏قَالُوا ‏ ‏كُنَّا ‏ ‏نُصَلِّي مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْمَغْرِبَ ثُمَّ نَنْصَرِفُ ‏ ‏فَنَتَرَامَى حَتَّى نَأْتِيَ دِيَارَنَا فَمَا ‏ ‏يَخْفَى عَلَيْنَا مَوَاقِعُ سِهَامِنَا ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, from Abū Bishr, from ʿAlī ibn Bilāl, from some people of the Anṣār who said: We used to pray Maghrib with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ then depart and shoot arrows at each other until we reached our homes, and we could still see where our arrows landed.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ بِلَالٍ اللَّيْثِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ ‏ ‏نَفَرٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَحَدَّثُونِي أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا ‏ ‏يُصَلُّونَ الْمَغْرِبَ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ يَنْطَلِقُونَ يَتَرَامَوْنَ لَا ‏ ‏يَخْفَى عَلَيْهِمْ مَوَاقِعُ سِهَامِهِمْ حَتَّى يَأْتُونَ دِيَارَهُمْ فِي أَقْصَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, he said: Abū ʿAwānah narrated to us, he said: Abū Bishr narrated to us, from ʿAlī ibn Bilāl al-Laythī who said: I prayed with a group of Companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, and they told me that they used to pray Maghrib with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ then go shooting arrows, and they could see where their arrows fell, until they reached their homes at the farthest point of Madīnah.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُشَيْرِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رِجَالٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَدْرَكَهُمْ ‏ ‏يَذْكُرُونَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِينَ ‏ ‏ظَهَرَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏وَصَارَتْ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرُ ‏ ‏لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَالْمُسْلِمِينَ ‏ ‏ضَعُفُوا ‏ ‏عَنْ عَمَلِهَا فَدَفَعُوهَا إِلَى ‏ ‏الْيَهُودِ ‏ ‏يَقُومُونَ عَلَيْهَا وَيُنْفِقُونَ عَلَيْهَا عَلَى أَنَّ لَهُمْ نِصْفَ مَا ‏ ‏يَخْرُجُ ‏ ‏مِنْهَا فَقَسَمَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى سِتَّةٍ وَثَلَاثِينَ سَهْمًا جَمَعَ كُلُّ سَهْمٍ مِائَةَ سَهْمٍ فَجَعَلَ نِصْفَ ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَكَانَ فِي ذَلِكَ النِّصْفِ سِهَامُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَسَهْمُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَعَهَا وَجَعَلَ النِّصْفَ الْآخَرَ لِمَنْ يَنْزِلُ بِهِ مِنْ الْوُفُودِ وَالْأُمُورِ ‏ ‏وَنَوَائِبِ ‏ ‏النَّاسِ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, he said: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Bushayr ibn Yasār, from men among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ whom he met, who mentioned that when the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ conquered Khaybar, it came under the possession of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and the Muslims. They were unable to cultivate it, so they entrusted it to the Jews to tend and invest in, on the condition that they would receive half of what it produced. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ divided it into thirty-six shares, each share comprising a hundred portions. He designated half of the total for the Muslims — and in that half were the shares of the Muslims and the share of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ among them. He designated the other half for visiting delegations, various affairs, and the needs of the people.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَجَّاجُ بْنُ أَرْطَاةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَفِظْنَا عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثِينَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَعْتَقَ ‏ ‏شِقْصًا ‏ ‏لَهُ فِي مَمْلُوكٍ ضَمِنَ بَقِيَّتَهُ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Ḥajjāj ibn Arṭāh narrated to us, from ʿAmr ibn Shuʿayb, from Saʿīd ibn al-Musayyib who said: We memorized from thirty Companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ that he said: 'Whoever frees a share (shiqṣ) that he owns in a slave, he bears responsibility for the remainder of the price.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ الْمُلَائِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْأَشَجِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ صَخْرٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏تَظَاهَرْتُ ‏ ‏مِنْ امْرَأَتِي ثُمَّ ‏ ‏وَقَعْتُ ‏ ‏بِهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ أُكَفِّرَ فَسَأَلْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَفْتَانِي بِالْكَفَّارَةِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Salām ibn Ḥarb al-Mulāʾī narrated to us, from Isḥāq ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Abī Farwah, from Bukayr ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn al-Ashajj, from Sulaymān ibn Yasār, from Salamah ibn Ṣakhr al-Zuraqī who said: I performed ẓihār from my wife, then had relations with her before making expiation. I asked the Prophet ﷺ, and he gave me the ruling of expiation (kaffārah).

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَنْبَأَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ صَخْرٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏تَظَاهَرْتُ ‏ ‏مِنْ امْرَأَتِي ثُمَّ ‏ ‏وَقَعْتُ ‏ ‏بِهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ أُكَفِّرَ فَسَأَلْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَفْتَانِي بِالْكَفَّارَةِ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq informed us, from Muḥammad ibn ʿAmr ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Sulaymān ibn Yasār, from Salamah ibn Ṣakhr al-Zuraqī who said: I performed ẓihār from my wife, then had relations with her before making expiation. I asked the Prophet ﷺ, and he gave me the ruling of expiation.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ صَخْرٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ امْرَأً قَدْ أُوتِيتُ مِنْ جِمَاعِ النِّسَاءِ مَا لَمْ يُؤْتَ غَيْرِي فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ رَمَضَانُ ‏ ‏تَظَاهَرْتُ ‏ ‏مِنْ امْرَأَتِي حَتَّى ‏ ‏يَنْسَلِخَ ‏ ‏رَمَضَانُ ‏ ‏فَرَقًا ‏ ‏مِنْ أَنْ أُصِيبَ فِي لَيْلَتِي شَيْئًا فَأَتَتَابَعُ فِي ذَلِكَ إِلَى أَنْ يُدْرِكَنِي النَّهَارُ وَأَنَا لَا أَقْدِرُ عَلَى أَنْ ‏ ‏أَنْزِعَ ‏ ‏فَبَيْنَا هِيَ تَخْدُمُنِي إِذْ تَكَشَّفَ لِي مِنْهَا شَيْءٌ ‏ ‏فَوَثَبْتُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهَا فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحْتُ ‏ ‏غَدَوْتُ ‏ ‏عَلَى قَوْمِي فَأَخْبَرْتُهُمْ خَبَرِي وَقُلْتُ لَهُمْ انْطَلِقُوا مَعِي إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأُخْبِرُهُ بِأَمْرِي فَقَالُوا لَا وَاللَّهِ لَا نَفْعَلُ نَتَخَوَّفُ أَنْ يَنْزِلَ فِينَا قُرْآنٌ أَوْ يَقُولَ فِينَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَقَالَةً يَبْقَى عَلَيْنَا عَارُهَا وَلَكِنْ اذْهَبْ أَنْتَ فَاصْنَعْ مَا بَدَا لَكَ قَالَ فَخَرَجْتُ فَأَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ خَبَرِي فَقَالَ لِي أَنْتَ بِذَاكَ فَقُلْتُ أَنَا بِذَاكَ فَقَالَ أَنْتَ بِذَاكَ فَقُلْتُ أَنَا بِذَاكَ قَالَ أَنْتَ بِذَاكَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ هَا أَنَا ذَا فَأَمْضِ فِيَّ حُكْمَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَإِنِّي صَابِرٌ لَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَعْتِقْ رَقَبَةً قَالَ فَضَرَبْتُ ‏ ‏صَفْحَةَ ‏ ‏رَقَبَتِي بِيَدِي وَقُلْتُ لَا وَالَّذِي بَعَثَكَ بِالْحَقِّ مَا أَصْبَحْتُ أَمْلِكُ غَيْرَهَا قَالَ فَصُمْ شَهْرَيْنِ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَهَلْ أَصَابَنِي مَا أَصَابَنِي إِلَّا فِي الصِّيَامِ قَالَ فَتَصَدَّقْ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ وَالَّذِي بَعَثَكَ بِالْحَقِّ لَقَدْ بِتْنَا لَيْلَتَنَا هَذِهِ ‏ ‏وَحْشَاءَ ‏ ‏مَا لَنَا عَشَاءٌ قَالَ اذْهَبْ إِلَى صَاحِبِ صَدَقَةِ ‏ ‏بَنِي زُرَيْقٍ ‏ ‏فَقُلْ لَهُ فَلْيَدْفَعْهَا إِلَيْكَ فَأَطْعِمْ عَنْكَ مِنْهَا ‏ ‏وَسْقًا ‏ ‏مِنْ تَمْرٍ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِينًا ثُمَّ اسْتَعِنْ بِسَائِرِهِ عَلَيْكِ وَعَلَى عِيَالِكَ قَالَ فَرَجَعْتُ إِلَى قَوْمِي فَقُلْتُ وَجَدْتُ عِنْدَكُمْ الضِّيقَ وَسُوءَ الرَّأْيِ وَوَجَدْتُ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏السَّعَةَ وَالْبَرَكَةَ قَدْ أَمَرَ لِي بِصَدَقَتِكُمْ فَادْفَعُوهَا لِي قَالَ فَدَفَعُوهَا إِلَيَّ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq informed us, from Muḥammad ibn ʿAmr ibn ʿAṭāʾ, from Sulaymān ibn Yasār, from Salamah ibn Ṣakhr al-Anṣārī who said: I was a man who had been given more desire for women than others. When Ramaḍān entered, I performed ẓihār from my wife until Ramaḍān would end, fearing that I might touch her at night and then be unable to stop until daylight overtook me. While she was serving me, something of her became uncovered, and I leapt upon her. When morning came, I went to my people and told them my story. I said: 'Come with me to the Prophet ﷺ so I can tell him my case.' They said: 'No, by Allāh! We fear that Qurʾān might be revealed about us, or the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ might say something about us whose shame would remain with us. But go yourself and do as you wish.' So I went out and came to the Prophet ﷺ and told him. He said: 'You did that?' I said: 'I did that.' He said: 'You did that?' I said: 'I did that.' He said: 'You did that?' I said: 'Yes, here I am. Execute Allāh's judgment upon me, for I am patient for it.' He said: 'Free a slave.' I struck the side of my neck with my hand and said: 'By the One who sent you with the truth, I own nothing but this neck of mine.' He said: 'Then fast two consecutive months.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allāh, did what befell me befall me except because of fasting?' He said: 'Then give charity.' I said: 'By the One who sent you with the truth, we spent last night hungry with nothing for dinner.' He said: 'Go to the collector of charity of Banū Zurayq and tell him to give it to you. Feed sixty poor people a wasq of dates on your behalf, then use the remainder for yourself and your family.' I returned to my people and said: 'With you I found narrowness and poor counsel, but with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ I found generosity and blessing. He has ordered that your charity be given to me, so hand it over to me.' So they handed it over.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنَا ‏ ‏بِالْأَبْوَاءِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏بِوَدَّانَ ‏ ‏فَأَهْدَيْتُ لَهُ مِنْ لَحْمِ حِمَارِ وَحْشٍ وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ ‏ ‏فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيَّ فَلَمَّا رَأَى فِي وَجْهِي الْكَرَاهَةَ قَالَ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِنَا رَدٌّ عَلَيْكَ وَلَكِنَّا حُرُمٌ ‏
en: Sufyān narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ passed by me while I was at al-Abwāʾ or Waddān. I gifted him meat of a wild donkey while he was in iḥrām. He returned it to me, and when he saw the displeasure on my face, he said: 'It is not that we are rejecting your gift, but we are in a state of iḥrām.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏حِمَى ‏ ‏إِلَّا لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ ‏
en: And I heard him say: 'There is no ḥimā (protected pasture) except for Allāh and His Messenger.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَسُئِلَ عَنْ أَهْلِ الدَّارِ مِنْ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ‏ ‏يُبَيَّتُونَ ‏ ‏فَيُصَابُ مِنْ نِسَائِهِمْ ‏ ‏وَذَرَارِيِّهِمْ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏هُمْ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ نَهَى عَنْ ذَلِكَ بَعْدُ ‏
en: And he was asked about the households of the polytheists that are raided at night, and their women and children are struck. He said: 'They are from them.' Then al-Zuhrī says: 'He later prohibited that.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏: ‏ ‏مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ اللَّيْثِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ أَهْدَى إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏بِالْأَبْوَاءِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏بِوَدَّانَ ‏ ‏حِمَارًا وَحْشِيًّا فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيْهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَأَى مَا فِي وَجْهِي قَالَ إِنَّا ‏ ‏لَمْ نَرُدَّ عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا أَنَّا حُرُمٌ ‏
en: He said: I read to ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī: Mālik ibn Anas, from Ibn Shihāb, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah al-Laythī, that he gifted the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ a wild donkey while he was at al-Abwāʾ or Waddān. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ returned it to him. When he saw what was on his face, he said: 'We only returned it to you because we are in a state of iḥrām.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏ابْنَ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قِيلَ لَهُ ‏ ‏لَوْ أَنَّ خَيْلًا ‏ ‏أَغَارَتْ ‏ ‏مِنْ اللَّيْلِ فَأَصَابَتْ مِنْ أَبْنَاءِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ قَالَ هُمْ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, he said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, he said: ʿAmr ibn Dīnār informed me that Ibn Shihāb informed him, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿUtbah, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ was asked: 'What if cavalry raids at night and strikes some of the children of the polytheists?' He said: 'They are from their fathers.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏حِمَى ‏ ‏إِلَّا لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿUtbah, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah who said: I heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ say: 'There is no ḥimā (protected pasture) except for Allāh and His Messenger.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا نُصِيبُ فِي ‏ ‏الْبَيَاتِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏ذَرَارِيِّ ‏ ‏الْمُشْرِكِينَ قَالَ هُمْ مِنْهُمْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿUtbah, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah who said: I said to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ: 'We strike in night raids some of the offspring of the polytheists.' He said: 'They are from them.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنَا ‏ ‏بِالْأَبْوَاءِ ‏ ‏فَأَهْدَيْتُ لَهُ حِمَارَ وَحْشٍ فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيَّ فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْكَرَاهِيَةَ فِي وَجْهِي قَالَ إِنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَيْسَ بِنَا رَدٌّ عَلَيْكَ وَلَكِنَّا حُرُمٌ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ passed by me while I was at al-Abwāʾ. I gifted him a wild donkey, but he returned it to me. When he saw the displeasure on my face, he said: 'It is not that we are rejecting your gift, but we are in a state of iḥrām.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏ابْنُ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏صَعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِي وَأَنَا ‏ ‏بِالْأَبْوَاءِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏بِوَدَّانَ ‏ ‏فَأَهْدَيْتُ لَهُ حِمَارَ وَحْشٍ فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيَّ فَلَمَّا رَأَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْكَرَاهِيَةَ فِي وَجْهِي قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِنَا رَدٌّ عَلَيْكَ وَلَكِنَّا حُرُمٌ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ ‏ ‏لِابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏الْحِمَارُ ‏ ‏عَقِيرٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا أَدْرِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ أَهْدَى إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِمَارَ وَحْشٍ وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَهُ ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh narrated to us, Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, he said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, he said: Ibn Shihāb informed me, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿUtbah, from ʿAbdullāh ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah who said: He passed by me while I was at al-Abwāʾ or Waddān, and I gifted him a wild donkey. He returned it to me, and when the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ saw the displeasure on my face, he said: 'It is not that we are rejecting your gift, but we are in a state of iḥrām.' I said to Ibn Shihāb: 'Was the donkey slaughtered (ʿaqīr)?' He said: 'I do not know.' Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Ibn Abī Dhiʾb informed us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUbaydullāh ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn ʿUtbah, from Ibn ʿAbbās, from al-Ṣaʿb ibn Jaththāmah, that he gifted the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ a wild donkey while he was in iḥrām — and he mentioned the hadith.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَالِكٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبَّادِ بْنِ تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏وَاضِعًا إِحْدَى رِجْلَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, he said: Mālik narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī — and ʿAbd al-Razzāq said: Maʿmar informed us, from al-Zuhrī — from ʿAbbād ibn Tamīm, from his uncle who said: I saw the Prophet ﷺ — ʿAbd al-Razzāq said in his narration — in the mosque, lying with one foot placed over the other.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏: ‏ ‏مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ يَحْيَى الْمَازِنِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ جَدَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏لِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَلْ تَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ تُرِيَنِي كَيْفَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَوَضَّأُ قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏نَعَمْ ‏ ‏فَدَعَا بِوَضُوءٍ فَأَفْرَغَ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ فَغَسَلَ يَدَهُ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ تَمَضْمَضَ ‏ ‏وَاسْتَنْثَرَ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ثُمَّ غَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ ثَلَاثًا ثُمَّ غَسَلَ يَدَيْهِ مَرَّتَيْنِ إِلَى الْمِرْفَقَيْنِ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رَأْسَهُ بِيَدَيْهِ فَأَقْبَلَ بِهِمَا ‏ ‏وَأَدْبَرَ ‏ ‏بَدَأَ بِمُقَدَّمِ رَأْسِهِ ثُمَّ ذَهَبَ بِهِمَا إِلَى قَفَاهُ ثُمَّ رَدَّهُمَا حَتَّى رَجَعَ إِلَى الْمَكَانِ الَّذِي بَدَأَ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ غَسَلَ رِجْلَيْهِ ‏
en: He said: I read to ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī: Mālik ibn Anas, from ʿAmr ibn Yaḥyā al-Māzinī, from his father, that his grandfather said to ʿAbdullāh ibn Zayd ibn ʿĀṣim — who was among the Companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ: 'Can you show me how the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ used to perform wuḍūʾ?' ʿAbdullāh ibn Zayd said: 'Yes.' He called for water and poured it over his hands, washing his hands twice. Then he rinsed his mouth and cleaned his nose three times. Then he washed his face three times. Then he washed his arms twice up to the elbows. Then he wiped his head with both hands, bringing them forward and back — starting from the front of his head, then taking them to the back of his neck, then bringing them back to where he started. Then he washed his feet.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبَّادِ بْنِ تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَسْقَى ‏ ‏وَحَوَّلَ ‏ ‏رِدَاءَهُ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd al-Anṣārī, from Abū Bakr ibn Muḥammad, from ʿAbbād ibn Tamīm, he said: ʿAbdullāh ibn Zayd said: The Prophet ﷺ went out and performed the rain prayer (istisqāʾ) and turned his cloak around.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبَّادِ بْنِ تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا بَيْنَ بَيْتِي وَمِنْبَرِي رَوْضَةٌ مِنْ رِيَاضِ الْجَنَّةِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from ʿAbdillāh ibn Abī Bakr, from ʿAbbād ibn Tamīm, from his uncle ʿAbdullāh ibn Zayd, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: 'What is between my house and my pulpit is a garden from the gardens of Paradise.'
